# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DEEP CONTEXT: ITERATIVE CODEBASE-CONTEXT LOOP (CONFIRM MODE)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Same workflow as deep_start-context-loop_auto.yaml, with user approval
# gates at init, before each parallel sweep, and before synthesis.
#
# YAML PREDICATE GRAMMAR CONTRACT:
# - Flow control is branch-label-driven (on_resume / on_fresh / on_A...);
#   `when:` is reserved for future typed predicates. See
#   `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/predicates/boolean-expr.ts`.
#
# GATE 3 CLASSIFICATION:
# - `resume` is a WRITE-PATH (iteration-NNN.md + JSONL appends), classified
#   write-path below so Gate 3 trigger extraction surfaces it.
#
# HOST-WRITES-STATE CONTRACT (deep-context invariant):
# - Every seat is a READ-ONLY analyzer returning structured findings; the
#   HOST is the ONLY writer of merged state (iterations, coverage-graph,
#   dashboard, Context Report). Gate-3-safe even with permissive CLI sandboxes.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

display.text:
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DEEP CONTEXT: ITERATIVE CODEBASE-CONTEXT LOOP (CONFIRM MODE)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Same workflow as deep_start-context-loop_auto.yaml, with user approval
# gates at init, before each parallel sweep, and before synthesis.
#
# YAML PREDICATE GRAMMAR CONTRACT:
# - Flow control is branch-label-driven (on_resume / on_fresh / on_A...);
#   `when:` is reserved for future typed predicates. See
#   `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/predicates/boolean-expr.ts`.
#
# GATE 3 CLASSIFICATION:
# - `resume` is a WRITE-PATH (iteration-NNN.md + JSONL appends), classified
#   write-path below so Gate 3 trigger extraction surfaces it.
#
# HOST-WRITES-STATE CONTRACT (deep-context invariant):
# - Every seat is a READ-ONLY analyzer returning structured findings; the
#   HOST is the ONLY writer of merged state (iterations, coverage-graph,
#   dashboard, Context Report). Gate-3-safe even with permissive CLI sandboxes.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
role: Deep Context Loop Manager
purpose: Iterative codebase-context gathering via parallel heterogeneous sweeps with convergence detection and user approval gates
action: Run deep-context loop with user confirmation at each phase transition
operating_mode:
  workflow: iterative_loop
  workflow_compliance: MANDATORY
  workflow_execution: interactive
  approvals: multi_gate
  tracking: progressive_context_state
  validation: convergence_detection

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OPERATING PRINCIPLES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
operating_principles:
  core: "Fresh per-iteration sweep + externalized state = no context degradation"
  approach: "Dispatch the WHOLE pool over ONE shared focus in parallel, show merged results, get approval, repeat or stop"
  mandate: "Seats are read-only analyzers; the host writes ALL merged state. Pause for the user at each gate."
  by_model_shared_scope: "Every seat sweeps the SAME current focus each iteration. Cross-executor AGREEMENT is the confidence signal — NOT disjoint-slice fan-out."

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# USER INPUTS (from setup phase)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
user_inputs:
  scope: "[SCOPE] - Target feature/area to gather codebase context for. REQUIRED."
  spec_folder: "[SPEC_FOLDER] - Spec folder path (or standalone run dir). REQUIRED."
  max_iterations: "[MAX_ITERATIONS] - Maximum loop iterations. Default: 8."
  convergence_threshold: "[CONVERGENCE_THRESHOLD] - Per-iteration new-agreement-eligible-findings floor. Default: 0.10."
  executor_pool: "[EXECUTOR_POOL] - Shared-scope pool (config.fanout.executors). Default: 2 native (native-only); CLI/combined via --executor/--executors."
  relevance_gate: "[RELEVANCE_GATE] - Min relevance to keep a finding (else lowConfidence). Default: 0.55 (deep_context_config.json)."
  agreement_min: "[AGREEMENT_MIN] - Min distinct executors for a finding to be agreement-eligible. Default: 2 (deep_context_config.json)."
  concurrency: "[CONCURRENCY] - Max parallel seats in the by-model sweep. Default: 4 (deep_context_config.json)."

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# CONTEXT LOADING
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
context_loading:
  trigger: "At workflow START, before phase_init"
  purpose: "Load prior context for the scope and buffer it until strategy.md exists"
  mcp_integration:
    tool: memory_context
    note: "Call MCP tools directly - NEVER through Code Mode"
    parameters: { input: "{scope}", mode: "focused", intent: "understand", includeContent: true }
  when_to_skip: ["User says 'skip context' or 'fresh start'"]
  behavior:
    if_context_found: "Store full results as prior_context plus a concise prior_context_summary; inject after strategy.md is created."
    if_no_context: "Set prior_context and prior_context_summary to 'None' and continue."

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SKILL REFERENCE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
skill_reference:
  skill: deep-context
  skill_md: .opencode/skills/deep-context/SKILL.md
  references:
    loop_protocol: .opencode/skills/deep-context/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md
    convergence: .opencode/skills/deep-context/references/convergence/convergence.md
  templates:
    config: .opencode/skills/deep-context/assets/deep_context_config.json
    report: .opencode/skills/deep-context/assets/context_report_template.md
  runtime:
    coverage_graph_db: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts
    convergence_script: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/convergence.cjs
    upsert_script: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/upsert.cjs
    fanout_run_script: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs
    fanout_merge_script: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs
    council_dispatch: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs
  prompt_framing: .opencode/skills/sk-prompt-small-model/SKILL.md

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# AGENT CONFIGURATION (native seat)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
agent_config:
  agent: deep-context
  agent_file: ".opencode/agents/deep-context.md"
  model: opus
  leaf_only: true
  read_only_analyzer: true
  tools: [Read, Grep, Glob, code_graph_query, code_graph_context]
  tool_call_budget: { target: 8, max: 12 }
  note: "Native seats are READ-ONLY. They return structured findings to the host; they never write merged state."

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STATE FILE PATHS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
state_paths:
  packet_dir: "{artifact_dir}"
  config: "{artifact_dir}/deep-context-config.json"
  state_log: "{artifact_dir}/deep-context-state.jsonl"
  strategy: "{artifact_dir}/deep-context-strategy.md"
  registry: "{artifact_dir}/findings-registry.json"
  dashboard: "{artifact_dir}/deep-context-dashboard.md"
  pause_sentinel: "{artifact_dir}/.deep-context-pause"
  lock_file: "{artifact_dir}/.deep-context.lock"
  prompt_dir: "{artifact_dir}/prompts"
  iteration_dir: "{artifact_dir}/iterations"
  iteration_pattern: "{artifact_dir}/iterations/iteration-{NNN}.md"
  seat_dir: "{artifact_dir}/seats"
  seat_pattern: "{artifact_dir}/seats/iter-{NNN}/{label}.json"
  delta_dir: "{artifact_dir}/deltas"
  delta_pattern: "{artifact_dir}/deltas/iter-{NNN}.jsonl"
  report_output: "{artifact_dir}/context-report.md"
  report_json_output: "{artifact_dir}/context-report.json"
  archive_root: "{artifact_archive_root}"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# WORKFLOW
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
workflow:

  step_resolve_artifact_root:
    description: "Resolve the context artifact directory from spec_folder (or standalone run dir)"
    note: "Root specs keep context artifacts in {spec_folder}/context/. Child phases keep local packet dirs in {spec_folder}/context/{packet}/. When no spec folder exists, use a standalone run dir and hand context-report.md to /speckit:plan."
    command: "node -e \"const { resolveArtifactRoot } = require('.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs'); console.log(JSON.stringify(resolveArtifactRoot('{spec_folder}', 'context')));\""
    outputs:
      - artifact_root_dir: "Resolved local context owner directory"
      - artifact_subfolder: "Local packet subfolder ({ownerSlug}-pt-{NN}), else null"
      - artifact_dir: "Resolved context packet directory for this target"
      - artifact_archive_root: "Resolved local context archive directory for this target"

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # PHASE 1: INITIALIZATION
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  phase_init:
    purpose: Create all state files and seed the frontier; gate before starting the loop
    steps:

      step_preflight_contract:
        action: "Validate required setup bindings and executable workflow contract before any file writes"
        verify:
          - required_values_present: [scope, spec_folder, max_iterations, convergence_threshold, executor_pool]
          - spec_folder_is_within: "specs/ or .opencode/specs/ (canonicalize alias roots before validation). A standalone run dir is allowed ONLY when no spec folder was named in the scope or derivable from it; FAIL closed if a standalone target is bound while the scope named or derived a spec folder (auto_mode_contract §1 fallback guard)"
          - pool_non_empty: "config.fanout.executors has at least one seat (warn when exactly one — no agreement signal)"
          - pool_mode: "config.fanout.mode == 'by-model-shared-scope'"
          - unresolved_placeholders: "FAIL if any user-facing template references placeholders not produced by earlier steps"
          - tool_contract: "FAIL if executable steps reference tools outside the command allowlist"
        on_fail: "Halt with STATUS=FAIL and report the missing binding or unsupported contract reference"

      step_create_directories:
        action: "Create spec folder and context packet directories if they don't exist"
        command: "mkdir -p {state_paths.prompt_dir} {state_paths.iteration_dir} {state_paths.delta_dir} {state_paths.seat_dir}"

      step_acquire_lock:
        action: "Acquire the single-writer advisory lock before session classification"
        command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/loop-lock.cjs acquire --lock {state_paths.lock_file} --packet {spec_folder}"
        on_acquire: "Capture ownerPid from the JSON output and reuse it for every release below. If acquired=false, stop and present recovery choices — another deep-context run already holds this packet."
        note: "Uses the deep-loop-runtime loop-lock helper (single source of truth: stale-lock reclaim, clean owner-scoped release). macOS/BSD locking is advisory — best-effort single-writer, not a hard mutex across host calls; stale-lock override is confirm-only or explicit recovery-only."
        on_halt:
          action: "Release the lock before any terminal halt branch returns control"
          command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock {state_paths.lock_file} --owner {captured_owner_pid}; failures here are logged but do not block the halt"
        on_cancel:
          action: "Release the lock on user cancel or pause-sentinel detection before sentinel processing"
          command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock {state_paths.lock_file} --owner {captured_owner_pid}; cancel completes regardless of cleanup result"
        on_workflow_exit:
          action: "Release the lock as the final step of any workflow exit path (success or failure)"
          command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock {state_paths.lock_file} --owner {captured_owner_pid}; idempotent — safe to invoke when already released"

      step_classify_session:
        action: "Classify the session before writing files"
        inspect:
          - "{state_paths.config}"
          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
        classify:
          fresh: "None of the three state artifacts exist"
          resume: "Config, JSONL, and strategy all exist and agree on scope/spec folder"
          completed-session: "Resume artifacts exist and config.status == 'complete'"
          invalid-state: "Any partial, missing, or contradictory combination"
        outputs:
          - session_state: "fresh | resume | completed-session | invalid-state"
        on_resume:
          gate3_classification: write-path
          gate3_rationale: "resume produces iteration-NNN.md writes and JSONL state-log appends"
          log: "Auto-resume: state artifacts are complete and consistent. Continue the same lineage."
          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"resumed","mode":"context","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{session_id}","lineageMode":"resume","continuedFromRun":{iteration_count},"generation":{generation},"archivedPath":null,"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
          skip_to: phase_loop
        on_restart:
          action: "Archive the current context packet under the local context_archive/ tree and start a new lineage segment."
          command: "if [ -d {state_paths.packet_dir} ]; then mkdir -p {state_paths.archive_root} && mv {state_paths.packet_dir} {state_paths.archive_root}/{timestamp_slug}; fi"
          set:
            lineage_mode: "restart"
            parent_session_id: "{prior_session_id}"
            generation: "{prior_generation + 1}"
          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"restarted","mode":"context","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{prior_session_id}","lineageMode":"restart","generation":{generation},"archivedPath":"{state_paths.archive_root}/{timestamp_slug}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
          skip_to: step_create_config
        on_completed_session:
          log: "Prior session already completed. Archive the completed context/ tree or start a fresh packet."
          halt: true
          message: "Completed deep-context packet detected. Archive or replace the existing context/ tree before starting a new session."
        on_invalid_state:
          halt: true
          message: "Existing deep-context state is incomplete or contradictory. Repair or archive the invalid context packet before continuing."
        on_fresh:
          action: "Proceed with a new session"

      step_seed_frontier:
        action: "Seed the SLICE frontier from the scope BEFORE the first sweep (never a whole-repo sweep)"
        instructions: |
          1. Extract anchors from {scope}: paths, symbols, error strings, domain terms.
          2. Expand via code_graph_query (2-5 word concepts) → blast-radius / calls → ranked SLICE nodes.
          3. Fall back to Glob+Grep over the anchors when the code graph is stale/absent.
          4. Cap the initial frontier so each iteration's focus is a tractable slice set.
        outputs:
          - frontier_slices_json: "Ranked SLICE node list (path, symbol, kind, rank), else []"
          - frontier_source: "code_graph | glob_grep_fallback"
        append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"frontier_seeded","mode":"context","source":"{frontier_source}","sliceCount":"{frontier_slice_count}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'

      step_create_config:
        action: "Create deep-context-config.json from template (carry the resolved pool + thresholds)"
        template: .opencode/skills/deep-context/assets/deep_context_config.json
        populate:
          scope: "{scope}"
          specFolder: "{spec_folder}"
          loopType: "context"
          executionMode: "confirm"
          maxIterations: "{max_iterations}"
          convergenceThreshold: "{convergence_threshold}"
          stuckThreshold: 2
          maxToolCallsPerIteration: 12
          relevanceGate: "{relevance_gate}"
          agreementMin: "{agreement_min}"
          fanout.mode: "by-model-shared-scope"
          fanout.concurrency: "{concurrency}"
          fanout.executors: "{executor_pool}"
          report.path: "context/context-report.md"
          report.jsonPath: "context/context-report.json"
          reducer.enabled: true
          reducer.registryFile: "context/findings-registry.json"
          reducer.dashboardFile: "context/deep-context-dashboard.md"
          createdAt: "{ISO_8601_NOW}"
          status: "initialized"
          lineage.sessionId: "{AUTO_SESSION_ID}"
          lineage.parentSessionId: null
          lineage.lineageMode: "new"
          lineage.generation: 1
          lineage.continuedFromRun: null
        output: "{state_paths.config}"

      step_create_state_log:
        action: "Initialize JSONL state log with config record"
        content: '{"type":"config","scope":"{scope}","loopType":"context","maxIterations":{max_iterations},"convergenceThreshold":{convergence_threshold},"relevanceGate":{relevance_gate},"agreementMin":{agreement_min},"fanout":{"mode":"by-model-shared-scope","concurrency":{concurrency}},"createdAt":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","specFolder":"{spec_folder}"}'
        output: "{state_paths.state_log}"

      step_create_strategy:
        action: "Create strategy file with the seeded frontier and the executor pool roster"
        populate:
          scope: "{scope}"
          frontier: "Render frontier_slices_json as the initial focus candidates (ranked SLICE list)"
          executor_pool: "Render the pool roster: one row per seat (label, kind, model, promptFramework)"
          known_context: "None"
          known_context_summary: "None"
          max_iterations: "{max_iterations}"
          convergence_threshold: "{convergence_threshold}"
          relevance_gate: "{relevance_gate}"
          agreement_min: "{agreement_min}"
        output: "{state_paths.strategy}"

      step_create_registry:
        action: "Initialize host-owned findings registry (merged, deduped, agreement-scored)"
        content: '{"reuseCandidates":[],"integrationPoints":[],"conventions":[],"dependencies":[],"gaps":[],"contradictions":[],"metrics":{"iterationsCompleted":0,"findings":0,"agreementEligible":0,"sliceCoverage":0,"reuseCatalogCoverage":0,"agreementRate":0,"relevanceFloor":0,"dependencyCompleteness":0,"convergenceScore":0}}'
        output: "{state_paths.registry}"

      step_enrich_strategy_context:
        action: "Ensure Known Context is written only after strategy.md exists"
        condition: "prior_context_found"
        edit: "{state_paths.strategy}"
        note: "Append or replace the Known Context section with prior_context_summary and retained detail from prior_context"

      # ─── APPROVAL GATE: Init ───
      gate_init_approval:
        type: approval_gate
        purpose: "Show initialization (seeded frontier + executor pool + thresholds) and get approval to start the loop"
        present: |
          ## Deep Context — Initialized

          **Scope**: {scope}
          **Spec folder**: {spec_folder}
          **Frontier**: {frontier_slice_count} SLICE nodes seeded via {frontier_source}
          **Executor pool (by-model-shared-scope)**: {pool_summary}
          **Thresholds**: maxIterations={max_iterations}, convergence={convergence_threshold}, relevanceGate={relevance_gate}, agreementMin={agreement_min}

          Each iteration sweeps the SAME focus with ALL seats in parallel; agreement is the confidence signal.

          Options:
          A) Start the loop with this frontier and pool
          B) Adjust the frontier slices, pool, or thresholds (provide changes)
          C) Cancel entirely
        wait_for: "User response"
        on_A: { set: { current_iteration: 1, stuck_count: 0, prev_agreement_eligible_count: 0, low_progress_streak: 0 }, proceed_to: phase_loop }
        on_B: { action: "Apply user adjustments to strategy.md and/or config", return_to: gate_init_approval }
        on_C: { action: "Cancel workflow", status: "CANCELLED" }

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # PHASE 2: PARALLEL-SWEEP ITERATION LOOP
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  phase_loop:
    purpose: Per iteration, gate, sweep the shared focus with the WHOLE pool in parallel, then merge + converge
    repeat: true
    max_repeats: "{max_iterations}"
    steps:

      step_read_state:
        action: "Read current state from JSONL, strategy, and registry"
        read:
          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
          - "{state_paths.config}"
          - "{state_paths.registry}"
        extract:
          - iteration_count: "Count lines where type === 'iteration'"
          - current_iteration: "iteration_count + 1"
          - current_focus: "Frontier slice set for this iteration from strategy.md 'Next Focus', else the seeded frontier"
          - new_agreement_eligible_last: "newAgreementEligible from the latest iteration record, else 'N/A'"
          - slice_coverage_latest: "sliceCoverage from latest iteration record, else 0"
          - agreement_rate_latest: "agreementRate from latest iteration record, else 0"
          - relevance_floor_latest: "relevanceFloor from latest iteration record, else 0"
          - uncovered_slices: "SLICE nodes without an outgoing COVERED_BY edge (recovery focus candidates)"
          - last_3_summaries: "Summarize the latest 3 iteration records as 'iter N: focus (newAgr, agreementRate)'"
          - latest_status: "Extract status from latest iteration record, else 'none yet'"

      step_check_pause_sentinel:
        action: "Check for pause sentinel file"
        check: "Does {state_paths.pause_sentinel} exist?"
        if_exists:
          log_event: '{"type":"event","event":"userPaused","mode":"context","run":{current_iteration},"stopReason":"userPaused","sentinelPath":"{state_paths.pause_sentinel}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
          message: "Context loop paused. Delete context/.deep-context-pause to resume."
          halt: true
        if_not_exists:
          proceed: true

      # ─── APPROVAL GATE: Pre-Iteration ───
      gate_pre_iteration:
        type: approval_gate
        purpose: "Show the planned shared focus + pool and get approval before dispatching the parallel sweep"
        present: |
          ## Iteration {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}

          **Shared focus (all seats analyze this same slice set)**: {current_focus}
          **Pool**: {pool_summary}
          **Last new agreement-eligible**: {new_agreement_eligible_last}
          **Last signals**: sliceCoverage={slice_coverage_latest}, agreementRate={agreement_rate_latest}, relevanceFloor={relevance_floor_latest}
          **Uncovered slices**: {uncovered_slices}

          Options:
          A) Proceed — dispatch the whole pool over this focus in parallel
          B) Change the shared focus to: [user provides new slice set]
          C) Stop the loop and synthesize now
          D) Cancel entirely
        wait_for: "User response"
        on_A: { proceed_to: step_render_seat_prompts }
        on_B: { action: "Update current_focus to user's choice", proceed_to: step_render_seat_prompts }
        on_C: { proceed_to: gate_pre_synthesis }
        on_D: { action: "Cancel workflow", status: "CANCELLED" }

      # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      # THE PARALLEL HETEROGENEOUS SWEEP
      # All seats analyze the SAME current_focus. native batch ‖ CLI pool
      # start TOGETHER and barrier-join. Seats are READ-ONLY; they write
      # only their own seats/iter-NNN/{label}.json. The host merges after.
      # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      step_render_seat_prompts:
        action: "Render ONE prompt per seat for the SHARED current focus, carrying the full lineage contract"
        ensure_dirs: "mkdir -p {state_paths.prompt_dir}/iter-{current_iteration} {state_paths.seat_dir}/iter-{current_iteration}"
        for_each: "seat in config.fanout.executors"
        contract_per_seat: |
          Every seat prompt MUST carry the four-part lineage contract (a seat told only "analyze"
          returns generic noise):
            1. gather-subject: "{scope}"
            2. scope/slice: the SHARED current_focus (this iteration's SLICE set) — identical for ALL seats
            3. known-context: prior_context_summary + already-confirmed reuse candidates (avoid re-finding)
            4. output schema: the structured-finding JSON the host expects (seat_output_schema below)
        prompt_framing:
          rule: "Apply each seat's promptFramework via sk-prompt-small-model (MiMo→COSTAR, MiniMax/DeepSeek→TIDD-EC). Native seats use no framework wrapper."
          loader: ".opencode/skills/sk-prompt-small-model/SKILL.md"
        seat_output_schema: |
          { "findings": [ { "unit_id": "sha256(path:symbol:kind)", "path": "...", "symbol": "...",
            "kind": "reuse_candidate|integration_point|convention|dependency|gap",
            "signature": "...", "reuse": "extend|compose|wrap|import", "evidence": "file:line",
            "relevance": 0.0, "notes": "..." } ] }
        output_path: "{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iter-{current_iteration}/{seat.label}.md"

<content>
1: ---
2: name: deep-context
3: description: "Iterative codebase-context-gathering deep loop. Runs a configurable pool over a shared scope in parallel (native-only by default; optional heterogeneous CLI seats) and synthesizes a reuse-first Context Report for planning/implementation. Use before /speckit:plan or /speckit:implement to map existing code, integration points, and conventions."
4: allowed-tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep]
5: version: 1.2.0
6: ---
7: 
8: <!-- Keywords: deep-context, context-gathering, codebase-context, reuse-catalog, by-model-parallel-sweep, agreement-merge, convergence-detection, coverage-graph, context-report, heterogeneous-executor-pool, pre-planning-context, relevance-gated-coverage -->
9: 
10: # Deep Context
11: 
12: Iterative, multi-model **codebase-context-gathering** loop. It sweeps the existing repository for the code relevant to a feature and synthesizes an implementation/planning-ready **Context Report** whose highest-value section is a **REUSE catalog** (existing functions/utilities to extend, cited by `file:symbol`). It is the "understand" loop that runs *before* `/speckit:plan` and `/speckit:implement`.
13: 
14: It is the fourth deep loop and the third consumer of `deep-loop-runtime` (alongside `deep-research` and `deep-review`). Unlike them it is **inward** (the codebase, not the web), and unlike a one-shot context lookup it is **convergence-gated** and **multi-model**: an operator-composed pool of executors sweeps the *same* scope in parallel, and **cross-executor agreement** drives finding confidence.
15: 
16: ---
17: 
18: ## 1. WHEN TO USE
19: 
20: ### Activation Triggers
21: 
22: **Use when**:
23: - You are about to plan or implement a feature and need a verified map of the existing code to reuse, the integration points to touch, and the conventions to follow.
24: - You want a Context Report that `/speckit:plan` can consume in place of its ad-hoc exploration dispatch.
25: - You want diverse model lenses on the same code (e.g. 2 native agents + MiMo + gpt + deepseek) with agreement-weighted confidence.
26: - A feature spans multiple modules and the blast radius is unclear before you plan.
27: 
28: **Keyword triggers**:
29: - `gather context`, `map the code for X`, `what existing code`, `what can I reuse`
30: - `pre-plan context`, `context loop`, `context sweep`, `deep context`
31: - `/deep:start-context-loop`
32: 
33: ### When NOT to Use
34: 
35: **Do not use for**:
36: - Outward/web knowledge discovery — use `deep-research`.
37: - Code audit / defect finding — use `deep-review`.
38: - Strategy deliberation between competing plans — use `deep-ai-council`.
39: - A quick one-shot context lookup — use the `@context` agent.
40: 
41: ---
42: 
43: ## 2. SMART ROUTING
44: 
45: > Pattern: aligned with the [sk-doc smart-router resilience template](../sk-doc/assets/skill/skill_smart_router.md).
46: 
47: ### Primary Detection Signal
48: 
49: ```text
50: Request contains "gather context" / "map the code" / "what existing code" / pre-plan understanding?
51:     |
52:     +-- /deep:start-context-loop invoked?         → ALWAYS load guides/quick_reference
53:     +-- "convergence" / "stop" / "saturation"?    → load convergence/* (signals, recovery, graph)
54:     +-- "state" / "jsonl" / "registry" / resume?  → load state/* (format, jsonl, outputs, reducer)
55:     +-- "reuse" / "REUSE catalog" / "report"?     → load state/state_outputs + context_report_template
56:     +-- "sweep" / "executor pool" / "seat"?       → load protocol/loop_protocol
57:     +-- Low-confidence or scope not stated?       → UNKNOWN_FALLBACK_CHECKLIST
58: ```
59: 
60: ### Phase Detection
61: 
62: ```text
63: REQUEST
64:     |
65:     +- STEP 0: Detect scope (feature / spec folder / query provided?)
66:     +- STEP 1: Score intents → loop setup, sweep dispatch, merge/agreement,
67:                convergence, state, coverage graph, or report synthesis
68:     +- STEP 2: Load intent-matched resources (guarded, existence-checked)
69:     +- Phase 1: Frontier seeding + parallel sweep (protocol/loop_protocol)
70:     +- Phase 2: Agreement merge + convergence (state/state_reducer_registry, convergence/*)
71:     +- Phase 3: Synthesis → Context Report (state/state_outputs, context_report_template)
72: ```
73: 
74: ### Resource Domains
75: 
76: The router discovers markdown resources recursively from `references/` and `assets/`, then applies intent scoring from `INTENT_SIGNALS`. References are organized into the same subfolder families as the sibling deep loops:
77: 
78: - `references/guides/` — operator cheat sheet (`quick_reference.md`); the ALWAYS baseline.
79: - `references/protocol/` — `loop_protocol.md`: iteration lifecycle, parallel sweep, host-writes-state, merge.
80: - `references/convergence/` — `convergence.md` (hub), `convergence_signals.md` (the 5 signals + weights + thresholds), `convergence_recovery.md` (blocked-stop / stuck recovery), `convergence_graph.md` (the `loop_type='context'` coverage-graph stop path).
81: - `references/state/` — `state_format.md` (packet hub), `state_jsonl.md` (record types), `state_outputs.md` (dashboard / Context Report), `state_reducer_registry.md` (reduce-state.cjs ownership + robustness).
82: - `assets/context_report_template.md` — Context Report schema (REUSE-catalog-first, pointers not bodies).
83: - `assets/deep_context_config.json` — config shape (scope, executor pool, concurrency, thresholds).
84: 
85: ### Resource Loading Levels
86: 
87: | Level       | When to Load                             | Resources                                          |
88: | ----------- | ---------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
89: | ALWAYS      | Every skill invocation                   | `references/guides/quick_reference.md`             |
90: | CONDITIONAL | Intent signals match                     | The intent-mapped `convergence/`, `protocol/`, `state/` refs + `context_report_template.md` |
91: | ON_DEMAND   | Explicit deep-dive keyword (see `ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS`) | The full reference set                  |
92: 
93: ### Smart Router Pseudocode
94: 
95: ```python
96: from pathlib import Path
97: 
98: SKILL_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
99: RESOURCE_BASES = (SKILL_ROOT / "references", SKILL_ROOT / "assets")
100: DEFAULT_RESOURCE = "references/guides/quick_reference.md"
101: 
102: # Deep-context intent signals: a top-level weight + flat keyword list per intent
103: # (canonical sibling shape — matches deep-research / deep-review).
104: INTENT_SIGNALS = {
105:     "LOOP_SETUP":       {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["deep context", "context loop", "gather context", "map the code", "pre-plan context", "frontier", "seed", "setup", "init"]},
106:     "SWEEP_DISPATCH":   {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["parallel sweep", "executor pool", "by-model", "heterogeneous", "seat", "fanout", "dispatch", "council seats", "multi-model"]},
107:     "MERGE_AGREEMENT":  {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["agreement", "merge", "dedup", "contradiction", "attribution", "confidence", "unit_id", "reducer", "registry"]},
108:     "CONVERGENCE":      {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["convergence", "stop", "saturation", "relevance gate", "agreement rate", "blocked stop", "STOP_ALLOWED", "recovery", "stuck"]},
109:     "STATE":            {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["state file", "jsonl", "dashboard", "packet", "findings-registry", "resume", "state log"]},
110:     "COVERAGE_GRAPH":   {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["coverage graph", "loop_type", "node kinds", "covered_by", "confirms", "graph signals", "upsert"]},
111:     "REPORT_SYNTHESIS": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["context report", "reuse catalog", "reuse", "integration point", "touch list", "synthesis", "report"]},
112: }
113: 
114: RESOURCE_MAP = {
115:     "LOOP_SETUP":       ["references/protocol/loop_protocol.md", "references/state/state_format.md"],
116:     "SWEEP_DISPATCH":   ["references/protocol/loop_protocol.md", "references/guides/quick_reference.md"],
117:     "MERGE_AGREEMENT":  ["references/state/state_reducer_registry.md", "references/convergence/convergence_signals.md"],
118:     "CONVERGENCE":      ["references/convergence/convergence.md", "references/convergence/convergence_signals.md", "references/convergence/convergence_recovery.md", "references/convergence/convergence_graph.md"],
119:     "STATE":            ["references/state/state_format.md", "references/state/state_jsonl.md", "references/state/state_outputs.md", "references/state/state_reducer_registry.md"],
120:     "COVERAGE_GRAPH":   ["references/convergence/convergence_graph.md"],
121:     "REPORT_SYNTHESIS": ["references/state/state_outputs.md", "assets/context_report_template.md"],
122: }
123: 
124: LOADING_LEVELS = {
125:     "ALWAYS": ["references/guides/quick_reference.md"],
126:     "ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS": ["full protocol", "all references", "complete reference", "resume deep context", "state log", "context/iterations", "blocked stop", "coverage graph", "reduce-state", "config schema"],
127:     "ON_DEMAND": [
128:         "references/protocol/loop_protocol.md",
129:         "references/convergence/convergence.md",
130:         "references/convergence/convergence_signals.md",
131:         "references/convergence/convergence_recovery.md",
132:         "references/convergence/convergence_graph.md",
133:         "references/state/state_format.md",
134:         "references/state/state_jsonl.md",
135:         "references/state/state_outputs.md",
136:         "references/state/state_reducer_registry.md",
137:         "assets/context_report_template.md",
138:     ],
139: }
140: 
141: UNKNOWN_FALLBACK_CHECKLIST = [
142:     "State the target feature or module to gather context for.",
143:     "Confirm whether a spec folder exists (or provide a standalone run path).",
144:     "Specify the executor pool (native × N, plus any CLI seats) or accept the default.",
145:     "Confirm verification expectations: what signals will indicate a useful Context Report?",
146: ]
147: 
148: AMBIGUITY_DELTA = 1
149: 
150: 
151: def _guard_in_skill(relative_path: str) -> str:
152:     resolved = (SKILL_ROOT / relative_path).resolve()
153:     resolved.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT)  # raises if outside skill root
154:     if resolved.suffix.lower() != ".md":
155:         raise ValueError(f"Only markdown resources are routable: {relative_path}")
156:     return resolved.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT).as_posix()
157: 
158: 
159: def discover_markdown_resources() -> set[str]:
160:     docs = []
161:     for base in RESOURCE_BASES:
162:         if base.exists():
163:             docs.extend(path for path in base.rglob("*.md") if path.is_file())
164:     return {doc.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT).as_posix() for doc in docs}
165: 
166: 
167: def load_if_available(relative_path: str, inventory, loaded, seen):
168:     guarded = _guard_in_skill(relative_path)
169:     if guarded in inventory and guarded not in seen:
170:         load(guarded)
171:         loaded.append(guarded)
172:         seen.add(guarded)
173: 
174: 
175: def score_intents(user_request: str) -> dict[str, int]:
176:     text = (user_request or "").lower()
177:     scores = {intent: 0 for intent in INTENT_SIGNALS}
178:     for intent, cfg in INTENT_SIGNALS.items():
179:         weight = cfg["weight"]
180:         for keyword in cfg["keywords"]:
181:             if keyword in text:
182:                 scores[intent] += weight
183:     return scores
184: 
185: 
186: def select_intents(scores: dict[str, int]) -> tuple[str, str | None]:
187:     ranked = sorted(scores.items(), key=lambda pair: pair[1], reverse=True)
188:     primary, primary_score = ranked[0]
189:     if primary_score == 0:
190:         return ("LOOP_SETUP", None)
191:     secondary, secondary_score = ranked[1]
192:     if secondary_score > 0 and (primary_score - secondary_score) <= AMBIGUITY_DELTA:
193:         return (primary, secondary)
194:     return (primary, None)
195: 
196: 
197: def route_deep_context_resources(user_request: str, task=None):
198:     inventory = discover_markdown_resources()
199:     scores = score_intents(user_request)
200:     primary, secondary = select_intents(scores)
201:     intents = [primary] + ([secondary] if secondary else [])
202: 
203:     loaded = []
204:     seen = set()
205: 
206:     # ALWAYS baseline (the operator cheat sheet)
207:     for relative_path in LOADING_LEVELS["ALWAYS"]:
208:         load_if_available(relative_path, inventory, loaded, seen)
209: 
210:     # No signal → return the baseline plus the disambiguation checklist
211:     if max(scores.values() or [0]) <= 0:
212:         return {
213:             "intents": intents,
214:             "intent_scores": scores,
215:             "load_level": "UNKNOWN_FALLBACK",
216:             "needs_disambiguation": True,
217:             "disambiguation_checklist": UNKNOWN_FALLBACK_CHECKLIST,
218:             "resources": loaded,
219:         }
220: 
221:     # CONDITIONAL: load intent-mapped resources
222:     for intent in intents:
223:         for relative_path in RESOURCE_MAP.get(intent, []):
224:             load_if_available(relative_path, inventory, loaded, seen)
225: 
226:     # ON_DEMAND: explicit deep-dive keywords pull the full reference set
227:     text = (user_request or "").lower()
228:     if any(keyword in text for keyword in LOADING_LEVELS["ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS"]):
229:         for relative_path in LOADING_LEVELS["ON_DEMAND"]:
230:             load_if_available(relative_path, inventory, loaded, seen)
231: 
232:     return {"intents": intents, "intent_scores": scores, "resources": loaded}
233: ```
234:

236: 
237: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS
238: 
239: > Depth lives in the references: [protocol/loop_protocol.md](references/protocol/loop_protocol.md) (full lifecycle), the [convergence/](references/convergence/convergence.md) family (stop contract, signals, recovery, graph), and the [state/](references/state/state_format.md) family (packet, JSONL, outputs, reducer). This section is the quick map.
240: 
241: **Process** (host-driven loop; the host = the orchestrating command/agent):
242: 
243: ```text
244: STEP 1: Seed the frontier
245:        ├─ Extract anchors from the target feature/query (paths, symbols, errors)
246:        ├─ Expand via code_graph_query (blast-radius/calls) into ranked SLICE nodes
247:        └─ Fall back to Glob + Grep when the code graph is stale or absent
248:        ↓
249: STEP 2: Parallel sweep (one iteration)
250:        ├─ native seats → parallel batch of @deep-context Task subagents
251:        ├─ CLI seats → deep-loop-runtime multi-seat-dispatch + per-kind spawn
252:        └─ Both groups start together; barrier-join (true heterogeneous parallelism)
253:        ↓
254: STEP 3: Merge + agreement (host only)
255:        ├─ Dedup findings by unit_id = sha256(path:symbol:kind)
256:        ├─ Union per-executor attribution; boost confidence by agreement count
257:        └─ Surface contradictions (CONTRADICTS edges); never silently resolve
258:        ↓
259: STEP 4: Persist + converge
260:        ├─ Host writes iteration state + coverage-graph events (loop_type='context')
261:        └─ convergence.cjs --loop-type context → CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED
262:        ↓
263: STEP 5: Synthesize
264:        └─ At stop, emit Context Report (context/context-report.md + .json)
265: ```
266: 
267: **Output**: a Context Report — REUSE catalog (verified `file:symbol` + signature + how-to-extend + confidence-by-agreement + freshness), integration points, touch list, conventions, pruned dependency subgraph, and gaps/unknowns. It ships **pointers, not source bodies** (the consumer pulls bodies just-in-time), which avoids context rot and stale-reference failure.
268: 
269: **Heterogeneous pool example** (opt-in Custom — NOT the default; the default pool is native-only, 2 seats): 2 native agents + 1 MiMo-v2.5-pro (cli-opencode) + 1 gpt (cli-codex) + 1 deepseek-v4-pro (cli-opencode), all sweeping the same scope in parallel; a reuse candidate confirmed by 3 of 5 executors outranks a single-executor find.
270: 
271: **Script**: `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` — the agreement-weighted context reducer. Reads the host-written state log + per-seat findings and produces the `findings-registry.json` and human-readable dashboard. Run from the repository root:
272: 
273: ```bash
274: node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/reduce-state.cjs <spec-folder>
275: ```
276: 
277: ### Runtime Mirrors (native seat dispatch)
278: 
279: The native `@deep-context` seat is dispatched **by name** (`agent: deep-context` in the loop YAML), resolved by each host runtime from its OWN `agents/` directory. It therefore lives as one canonical source plus two runtime mirrors that must stay in sync:
280: 
281: | Runtime | File | Frontmatter |
282: |---------|------|-------------|
283: | OpenCode | `.opencode/agents/deep-context.md` | **canonical source** — `mode: subagent` + `permission:` block |
284: | Claude Code | `.claude/agents/deep-context.md` | mirror — `tools:` allow-list (read-only), same body |
285: | Codex | `.codex/agents/deep-context.toml` | mirror — `developer_instructions = '''…'''` + `# Converted from:` header + `sandbox_mode = "read-only"`, same body |
286: 
287: The body is identical across all three; only the frontmatter format differs. The command and loop YAML are **shared** — the `.claude/` and `.codex/` `commands`/`prompts`/`skills` directories are symlinks to `.opencode/` — so they intentionally reference the canonical `.opencode/` paths and dispatch by name; do NOT fork them per runtime. If a mirror is missing for a runtime, the native seats silently fail to dispatch there (the CLI seats still run, but the cross-executor agreement signal degrades). When you edit the canonical agent, re-sync both mirrors in the same change.
288: 
289: ---
290: 
291: ## 4. RULES
292: 
293: ### ALWAYS
294: 
295: 1. **Treat every executor seat as a read-only analyzer.** The host writes all state (iteration files, coverage-graph, the merged report). Sub-agents must never write the merged report.
296: 2. **Carry the full lineage prompt contract to every seat**: gather-subject + scope/slice + known-context + output schema. A seat told only "analyze" returns generic noise.
297: 3. **Apply each model's prompt framework via `sk-prompt-small-model`** (e.g. MiMo → COSTAR, MiniMax → TIDD-EC) using the lineage `promptFramework` field.
298: 4. **Verify every cited `file:symbol` against the code graph** before it enters the report; label anything unverified. A stale reference is worse than omission.
299: 5. **Honor the cli-* skill contracts for dispatch** (model id form, `</dev/null` for opencode, omit top-level `--agent`). Read the relevant `cli-X/SKILL.md` before composing any CLI prompt.
300: 6. **Ship pointers + signatures, not source bodies.** Context rot begins when full source is pasted into reports.
301: 7. **The host applies the deep-loop-runtime robustness layer** (shared with `deep-research` and `deep-review`): state writes are atomic (temp+fsync+rename via `writeStateAtomic`), the JSONL state log is repaired before each reduce (`repairJsonlTail`), each seat's output is validated before merge (`post-dispatch-validate`, surfacing `seatValidationWarnings`), a single-writer advisory loop-lock is held via `scripts/loop-lock.cjs` for the duration of the session, and CLI seats are dispatched with the runtime recursion-guard env so no seat can launch a nested deep-context loop.
302: 8. **Keep the native agent's runtime mirrors in sync** — when editing `.opencode/agents/deep-context.md` (canonical), update `.claude/agents/deep-context.md` and `.codex/agents/deep-context.toml` in the same change. The loop dispatches the native seat by name from each runtime's own `agents/` dir, so a missing mirror means native seats silently fail to dispatch in that runtime. See **Runtime Mirrors** in §3.
303: 
304: ### NEVER
305: 
306: 1. **Never sweep the whole repo** or paste raw source into the report. Seed a focused frontier; expand only within the blast radius.
307: 2. **Never count "files visited" as progress.** Only relevance-gated, agreement-eligible findings advance coverage.
308: 3. **Never let a seat write outside its own artifact dir** or mutate `spec.md` (that is `/speckit:plan`'s job).
309: 4. **Never register new MCP tools.** The runtime is MCP-free by design.
310: 
311: ### ESCALATE IF
312: 
313: 1. **A provider in the pool is not authenticated** or the code graph is unavailable and Glob/Grep fallback is insufficient to seed a meaningful frontier.
314: 2. **Executors contradict on a finding's contract** (different signature or reuse verb for the same `unit_id`) and the host cannot reconcile from evidence — surface both sides to the operator.
315: 3. **The loop cannot reach the agreement/relevance gates within the iteration cap** — surface partial findings plus the blocking gate and let the operator decide whether to extend the cap or accept the partial report.
316:

368: 
369: ## 6. SUCCESS CRITERIA
370: 
371: **Task complete when**:
372: - A Context Report exists at `{spec_folder}/context/context-report.md` with a REUSE catalog whose entries are code-graph-verified (no unlabeled stale refs).
373: - Convergence stopped via `STOP_ALLOWED` (coverage + relevance + agreement gates passed) or a recorded cap/blocked stop with partial findings and the blocking gate named.
374: - Per-executor attribution + agreement counts are recorded for every finding in `context/findings-registry.json`.
375: 
376: **Quality gates**:
377: - `agreementRate >= 0.50` — findings are multi-model-confirmed, not single-seat noise.
378: - `relevanceFloor >= 0.50` — the loop collected focused context, not tangential files.
379: - `sliceCoverage >= 0.70` — the defined scope was swept, not partially skimmed.
380: 
381: ---
382: 
383: ## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS
384: 
385: **Triggers**: pre-planning context gathering; requests matching "gather context", "map the code for X", "what can I reuse for X", `/deep:start-context-loop`.
386: 
387: **Pairs with**:
388: - `deep-loop-runtime` — coverage-graph (`loop_type='context'`), convergence script, parallel seat dispatch, executor config.
389: - `sk-prompt-small-model` — per-model prompt framing for the heterogeneous pool.
390: - `cli-opencode` / `cli-codex` / `cli-claude-code` / `cli-devin` — CLI seat dispatch contracts.
391: - `system-code-graph` — frontier seeding + reference verification.
392: - `/speckit:plan` and `/speckit:implement` — downstream consumers of the Context Report.
393: 
394: **Packet layout** (`{spec_folder}/context/`):
395: - `deep-context-config.json` — run config.
396: - `deep-context-state.jsonl` — append-only state log.
397: - `deep-context-strategy.md` — focus and scope notes.
398: - `iterations/iteration-NNN.md` — per-iteration summaries.
399: - `findings-registry.json` — agreement-weighted findings (reducer-owned).
400: - `deep-context-dashboard.md` — auto-generated progress view (reducer-owned).
401: - `context-report.md` + `context-report.json` — the deliverable.
402: 
403: If the user named a spec folder, or one is derivable from the scope (e.g. a spec-folder path inside the scope text), the packet MUST live at `{spec_folder}/context/`. A standalone run dir is used ONLY when no spec folder is named or derivable — fail-closed, never as a default when a folder is identifiable (see `auto_mode_contract` §1 source 3 + fallback guard). In the standalone case the host hands the report path to `/speckit:plan`.
404:

407: ## 8. QUICK REFERENCE
408: 
409: | Item | Value |
410: |------|-------|
411: | Command | `/deep:start-context-loop` (`:auto` / `:confirm`) |
412: | Agent | `@deep-context` (LEAF, read-only analyzer seat) |
413: | Loop type | `context` in `deep-loop-runtime` coverage-graph + `convergence.cjs` |

415: | Convergence signals | `sliceCoverage`, `reuseCatalogCoverage`, `agreementRate` (guard), `relevanceFloor` (guard), `dependencyCompleteness` |
416: | Packet | `{spec_folder}/context/` (config, state, iterations, `context-report.md`) |
417: | Default pool | 2 native `@deep-context` seats (native-only), over the shared scope; add `--executor` for CLI/heterogeneous |
418: | `relevanceGate` | 0.55 (findings below route to `lowConfidence`) |
419: | `agreementMin` | 2 distinct executors for agreement-eligibility |
420: | `maxIterations` | 8 |
421: | `convergenceThreshold` | 0.10 (new agreement-eligible findings per iteration at saturation) |
422: | `fanout.mode` | `by-model-shared-scope` |
423: | Stop score weights | reuse-first (0.30 reuseCatalog, 0.25 agreement, 0.20 slice, 0.15 relevance, 0.10 dependency) |
424: | Runtime robustness | Inherits deep-loop-runtime durability layer: atomic-state, jsonl-repair, post-dispatch-validate, loop-lock (`scripts/loop-lock.cjs`), executor-audit recursion guard |

428: ## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES
429: 
430: The router discovers reference, asset, and script docs dynamically. Start with `references/guides/quick_reference.md` (the ALWAYS baseline) and `references/protocol/loop_protocol.md`, then load intent-specific `convergence/` and `state/` resources according to Section 2.
431: 
432: Scripts: `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` — the agreement-weighted context reducer; run it from the repository root with a spec-folder argument.
433: 
434: Related skills: `deep-research` for outward/web investigation (sibling loop whose structure this mirrors), `deep-review` for code audit, `deep-loop-runtime` for shared executor and coverage-graph infrastructure, `sk-prompt-small-model` for per-model prompt framing, and `system-spec-kit` when packet documentation or memory continuity applies.

(End of file - total 434 lines)
</content>

# Deep Context

Iterative, multi-model **codebase-context-gathering** loop. It sweeps the existing repository for the code relevant to a feature and synthesizes an implementation/planning-ready **Context Report** whose highest-value section is a **REUSE catalog** (existing functions/utilities to extend, cited by `file:symbol`). It is the "understand" loop that runs *before* `/speckit:plan` and `/speckit:implement`.

It is the fourth deep loop and the third consumer of `deep-loop-runtime` (alongside `deep-research` and `deep-review`). Unlike them it is **inward** (the codebase, not the web), and unlike a one-shot context lookup it is **convergence-gated** and **multi-model**: an operator-composed pool of executors sweeps the *same* scope in parallel, and **cross-executor agreement** drives finding confidence.

---

## 1. WHEN TO USE

### Activation Triggers

**Use when**:
- You are about to plan or implement a feature and need a verified map of the existing code to reuse, the integration points to touch, and the conventions to follow.
- You want a Context Report that `/speckit:plan` can consume in place of its ad-hoc exploration dispatch.
- You want diverse model lenses on the same code (e.g. 2 native agents + MiMo + gpt + deepseek) with agreement-weighted confidence.
- A feature spans multiple modules and the blast radius is unclear before you plan.

**Keyword triggers**:
- `gather context`, `map the code for X`, `what existing code`, `what can I reuse`
- `pre-plan context`, `context loop`, `context sweep`, `deep context`
- `/deep:start-context-loop`

### When NOT to Use

**Do not use for**:
- Outward/web knowledge discovery — use `deep-research`.
- Code audit / defect finding — use `deep-review`.
- Strategy deliberation between competing plans — use `deep-ai-council`.
- A quick one-shot context lookup — use the `@context` agent.

---

## 2. SMART ROUTING

> Pattern: aligned with the [sk-doc smart-router resilience template](../sk-doc/assets/skill/skill_smart_router.md).

### Primary Detection Signal

```text
Request contains "gather context" / "map the code" / "what existing code" / pre-plan understanding?
    |
    +-- /deep:start-context-loop invoked?         → ALWAYS load guides/quick_reference
    +-- "convergence" / "stop" / "saturation"?    → load convergence/* (signals, recovery, graph)
    +-- "state" / "jsonl" / "registry" / resume?  → load state/* (format, jsonl, outputs, reducer)
    +-- "reuse" / "REUSE catalog" / "report"?     → load state/state_outputs + context_report_template
    +-- "sweep" / "executor pool" / "seat"?       → load protocol/loop_protocol
    +-- Low-confidence or scope not stated?       → UNKNOWN_FALLBACK_CHECKLIST
```

### Phase Detection

```text
REQUEST
    |
    +- STEP 0: Detect scope (feature / spec folder / query provided?)
    +- STEP 1: Score intents → loop setup, sweep dispatch, merge/agreement,
               convergence, state, coverage graph, or report synthesis
    +- STEP 2: Load intent-matched resources (guarded, existence-checked)
    +- Phase 1: Frontier seeding + parallel sweep (protocol/loop_protocol)
    +- Phase 2: Agreement merge + convergence (state/state_reducer_registry, convergence/*)
    +- Phase 3: Synthesis → Context Report (state/state_outputs, context_report_template)
```

### Resource Domains

The router discovers markdown resources recursively from `references/` and `assets/`, then applies intent scoring from `INTENT_SIGNALS`. References are organized into the same subfolder families as the sibling deep loops:

- `references/guides/` — operator cheat sheet (`quick_reference.md`); the ALWAYS baseline.
- `references/protocol/` — `loop_protocol.md`: iteration lifecycle, parallel sweep, host-writes-state, merge.
- `references/convergence/` — `convergence.md` (hub), `convergence_signals.md` (the 5 signals + weights + thresholds), `convergence_recovery.md` (blocked-stop / stuck recovery), `convergence_graph.md` (the `loop_type='context'` coverage-graph stop path).
- `references/state/` — `state_format.md` (packet hub), `state_jsonl.md` (record types), `state_outputs.md` (dashboard / Context Report), `state_reducer_registry.md` (reduce-state.cjs ownership + robustness).
- `assets/context_report_template.md` — Context Report schema (REUSE-catalog-first, pointers not bodies).
- `assets/deep_context_config.json` — config shape (scope, executor pool, concurrency, thresholds).

### Resource Loading Levels

| Level       | When to Load                             | Resources                                          |
| ----------- | ---------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| ALWAYS      | Every skill invocation                   | `references/guides/quick_reference.md`             |
| CONDITIONAL | Intent signals match                     | The intent-mapped `convergence/`, `protocol/`, `state/` refs + `context_report_template.md` |
| ON_DEMAND   | Explicit deep-dive keyword (see `ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS`) | The full reference set                  |

### Smart Router Pseudocode

```python
from pathlib import Path

SKILL_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
RESOURCE_BASES = (SKILL_ROOT / "references", SKILL_ROOT / "assets")
DEFAULT_RESOURCE = "references/guides/quick_reference.md"

# Deep-context intent signals: a top-level weight + flat keyword list per intent
# (canonical sibling shape — matches deep-research / deep-review).
INTENT_SIGNALS = {
    "LOOP_SETUP":       {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["deep context", "context loop", "gather context", "map the code", "pre-plan context", "frontier", "seed", "setup", "init"]},
    "SWEEP_DISPATCH":   {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["parallel sweep", "executor pool", "by-model", "heterogeneous", "seat", "fanout", "dispatch", "council seats", "multi-model"]},
    "MERGE_AGREEMENT":  {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["agreement", "merge", "dedup", "contradiction", "attribution", "confidence", "unit_id", "reducer", "registry"]},
    "CONVERGENCE":      {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["convergence", "stop", "saturation", "relevance gate", "agreement rate", "blocked stop", "STOP_ALLOWED", "recovery", "stuck"]},
    "STATE":            {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["state file", "jsonl", "dashboard", "packet", "findings-registry", "resume", "state log"]},
    "COVERAGE_GRAPH":   {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["coverage graph", "loop_type", "node kinds", "covered_by", "confirms", "graph signals", "upsert"]},
    "REPORT_SYNTHESIS": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["context report", "reuse catalog", "reuse", "integration point", "touch list", "synthesis", "report"]},
}

RESOURCE_MAP = {
    "LOOP_SETUP":       ["references/protocol/loop_protocol.md", "references/state/state_format.md"],
    "SWEEP_DISPATCH":   ["references/protocol/loop_protocol.md", "references/guides/quick_reference.md"],
    "MERGE_AGREEMENT":  ["references/state/state_reducer_registry.md", "references/convergence/convergence_signals.md"],
    "CONVERGENCE":      ["references/convergence/convergence.md", "references/convergence/convergence_signals.md", "references/convergence/convergence_recovery.md", "references/convergence/convergence_graph.md"],
    "STATE":            ["references/state/state_format.md", "references/state/state_jsonl.md", "references/state/state_outputs.md", "references/state/state_reducer_registry.md"],
    "COVERAGE_GRAPH":   ["references/convergence/convergence_graph.md"],
    "REPORT_SYNTHESIS": ["references/state/state_outputs.md", "assets/context_report_template.md"],
}

LOADING_LEVELS = {
    "ALWAYS": ["references/guides/quick_reference.md"],
    "ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS": ["full protocol", "all references", "complete reference", "resume deep context", "state log", "context/iterations", "blocked stop", "coverage graph", "reduce-state", "config schema"],
    "ON_DEMAND": [
        "references/protocol/loop_protocol.md",
        "references/convergence/convergence.md",
        "references/convergence/convergence_signals.md",
        "references/convergence/convergence_recovery.md",
        "references/convergence/convergence_graph.md",
        "references/state/state_format.md",
        "references/state/state_jsonl.md",
        "references/state/state_outputs.md",
        "references/state/state_reducer_registry.md",
        "assets/context_report_template.md",
    ],
}

UNKNOWN_FALLBACK_CHECKLIST = [
    "State the target feature or module to gather context for.",
    "Confirm whether a spec folder exists (or provide a standalone run path).",
    "Specify the executor pool (native × N, plus any CLI seats) or accept the default.",
    "Confirm verification expectations: what signals will indicate a useful Context Report?",
]

AMBIGUITY_DELTA = 1


def _guard_in_skill(relative_path: str) -> str:
    resolved = (SKILL_ROOT / relative_path).resolve()
    resolved.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT)  # raises if outside skill root
    if resolved.suffix.lower() != ".md":
        raise ValueError(f"Only markdown resources are routable: {relative_path}")
    return resolved.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT).as_posix()


def discover_markdown_resources() -> set[str]:
    docs = []
    for base in RESOURCE_BASES:
        if base.exists():
            docs.extend(path for path in base.rglob("*.md") if path.is_file())
    return {doc.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT).as_posix() for doc in docs}


def load_if_available(relative_path: str, inventory, loaded, seen):
    guarded = _guard_in_skill(relative_path)
    if guarded in inventory and guarded not in seen:
        load(guarded)
        loaded.append(guarded)
        seen.add(guarded)


def score_intents(user_request: str) -> dict[str, int]:
    text = (user_request or "").lower()
    scores = {intent: 0 for intent in INTENT_SIGNALS}
    for intent, cfg in INTENT_SIGNALS.items():
        weight = cfg["weight"]
        for keyword in cfg["keywords"]:
            if keyword in text:
                scores[intent] += weight
    return scores


def select_intents(scores: dict[str, int]) -> tuple[str, str | None]:
    ranked = sorted(scores.items(), key=lambda pair: pair[1], reverse=True)
    primary, primary_score = ranked[0]
    if primary_score == 0:
        return ("LOOP_SETUP", None)
    secondary, secondary_score = ranked[1]
    if secondary_score > 0 and (primary_score - secondary_score) <= AMBIGUITY_DELTA:
        return (primary, secondary)
    return (primary, None)


def route_deep_context_resources(user_request: str, task=None):
    inventory = discover_markdown_resources()
    scores = score_intents(user_request)
    primary, secondary = select_intents(scores)
    intents = [primary] + ([secondary] if secondary else [])

    loaded = []
    seen = set()

    # ALWAYS baseline (the operator cheat sheet)
    for relative_path in LOADING_LEVELS["ALWAYS"]:
        load_if_available(relative_path, inventory, loaded, seen)

    # No signal → return the baseline plus the disambiguation checklist
    if max(scores.values() or [0]) <= 0:
        return {
            "intents": intents,
            "intent_scores": scores,
            "load_level": "UNKNOWN_FALLBACK",
            "needs_disambiguation": True,
            "disambiguation_checklist": UNKNOWN_FALLBACK_CHECKLIST,
            "resources": loaded,
        }

    # CONDITIONAL: load intent-mapped resources
    for intent in intents:
        for relative_path in RESOURCE_MAP.get(intent, []):
            load_if_available(relative_path, inventory, loaded, seen)

    # ON_DEMAND: explicit deep-dive keywords pull the full reference set
    text = (user_request or "").lower()
    if any(keyword in text for keyword in LOADING_LEVELS["ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS"]):
        for relative_path in LOADING_LEVELS["ON_DEMAND"]:
            load_if_available(relative_path, inventory, loaded, seen)

    return {"intents": intents, "intent_scores": scores, "resources": loaded}
```

---

## 3. HOW IT WORKS

> Depth lives in the references: [protocol/loop_protocol.md](references/protocol/loop_protocol.md) (full lifecycle), the [convergence/](references/convergence/convergence.md) family (stop contract, signals, recovery, graph), and the [state/](references/state/state_format.md) family (packet, JSONL, outputs, reducer). This section is the quick map.

**Process** (host-driven loop; the host = the orchestrating command/agent):

```text
STEP 1: Seed the frontier
       ├─ Extract anchors from the target feature/query (paths, symbols, errors)
       ├─ Expand via code_graph_query (blast-radius/calls) into ranked SLICE nodes
       └─ Fall back to Glob + Grep when the code graph is stale or absent
       ↓
STEP 2: Parallel sweep (one iteration)
       ├─ native seats → parallel batch of @deep-context Task subagents
       ├─ CLI seats → deep-loop-runtime multi-seat-dispatch + per-kind spawn
       └─ Both groups start together; barrier-join (true heterogeneous parallelism)
       ↓
STEP 3: Merge + agreement (host only)
       ├─ Dedup findings by unit_id = sha256(path:symbol:kind)
       ├─ Union per-executor attribution; boost confidence by agreement count
       └─ Surface contradictions (CONTRADICTS edges); never silently resolve
       ↓
STEP 4: Persist + converge
       ├─ Host writes iteration state + coverage-graph events (loop_type='context')
       └─ convergence.cjs --loop-type context → CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED
       ↓
STEP 5: Synthesize
       └─ At stop, emit Context Report (context/context-report.md + .json)
```

**Output**: a Context Report — REUSE catalog (verified `file:symbol` + signature + how-to-extend + confidence-by-agreement + freshness), integration points, touch list, conventions, pruned dependency subgraph, and gaps/unknowns. It ships **pointers, not source bodies** (the consumer pulls bodies just-in-time), which avoids context rot and stale-reference failure.

**Heterogeneous pool example** (opt-in Custom — NOT the default; the default pool is native-only, 2 seats): 2 native agents + 1 MiMo-v2.5-pro (cli-opencode) + 1 gpt (cli-codex) + 1 deepseek-v4-pro (cli-opencode), all sweeping the same scope in parallel; a reuse candidate confirmed by 3 of 5 executors outranks a single-executor find.

**Script**: `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` — the agreement-weighted context reducer. Reads the host-written state log + per-seat findings and produces the `findings-registry.json` and human-readable dashboard. Run from the repository root:

```bash
node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/reduce-state.cjs <spec-folder>
```

### Runtime Mirrors (native seat dispatch)

The native `@deep-context` seat is dispatched **by name** (`agent: deep-context` in the loop YAML), resolved by each host runtime from its OWN `agents/` directory. It therefore lives as one canonical source plus two runtime mirrors that must stay in sync:

| Runtime | File | Frontmatter |
|---------|------|-------------|
| OpenCode | `.opencode/agents/deep-context.md` | **canonical source** — `mode: subagent` + `permission:` block |
| Claude Code | `.claude/agents/deep-context.md` | mirror — `tools:` allow-list (read-only), same body |
| Codex | `.codex/agents/deep-context.toml` | mirror — `developer_instructions = '''…'''` + `# Converted from:` header + `sandbox_mode = "read-only"`, same body |

The body is identical across all three; only the frontmatter format differs. The command and loop YAML are **shared** — the `.claude/` and `.codex/` `commands`/`prompts`/`skills` directories are symlinks to `.opencode/` — so they intentionally reference the canonical `.opencode/` paths and dispatch by name; do NOT fork them per runtime. If a mirror is missing for a runtime, the native seats silently fail to dispatch there (the CLI seats still run, but the cross-executor agreement signal degrades). When you edit the canonical agent, re-sync both mirrors in the same change.

---

## 4. RULES

### ALWAYS

1. **Treat every executor seat as a read-only analyzer.** The host writes all state (iteration files, coverage-graph, the merged report). Sub-agents must never write the merged report.
2. **Carry the full lineage prompt contract to every seat**: gather-subject + scope/slice + known-context + output schema. A seat told only "analyze" returns generic noise.
3. **Apply each model's prompt framework via `sk-prompt-small-model`** (e.g. MiMo → COSTAR, MiniMax → TIDD-EC) using the lineage `promptFramework` field.
4. **Verify every cited `file:symbol` against the code graph** before it enters the report; label anything unverified. A stale reference is worse than omission.
5. **Honor the cli-* skill contracts for dispatch** (model id form, `</dev/null` for opencode, omit top-level `--agent`). Read the relevant `cli-X/SKILL.md` before composing any CLI prompt.
6. **Ship pointers + signatures, not source bodies.** Context rot begins when full source is pasted into reports.
7. **The host applies the deep-loop-runtime robustness layer** (shared with `deep-research` and `deep-review`): state writes are atomic (temp+fsync+rename via `writeStateAtomic`), the JSONL state log is repaired before each reduce (`repairJsonlTail`), each seat's output is validated before merge (`post-dispatch-validate`, surfacing `seatValidationWarnings`), a single-writer advisory loop-lock is held via `scripts/loop-lock.cjs` for the duration of the session, and CLI seats are dispatched with the runtime recursion-guard env so no seat can launch a nested deep-context loop.
8. **Keep the native agent's runtime mirrors in sync** — when editing `.opencode/agents/deep-context.md` (canonical), update `.claude/agents/deep-context.md` and `.codex/agents/deep-context.toml` in the same change. The loop dispatches the native seat by name from each runtime's own `agents/` dir, so a missing mirror means native seats silently fail to dispatch in that runtime. See **Runtime Mirrors** in §3.

### NEVER

1. **Never sweep the whole repo** or paste raw source into the report. Seed a focused frontier; expand only within the blast radius.
2. **Never count "files visited" as progress.** Only relevance-gated, agreement-eligible findings advance coverage.
3. **Never let a seat write outside its own artifact dir** or mutate `spec.md` (that is `/speckit:plan`'s job).
4. **Never register new MCP tools.** The runtime is MCP-free by design.

### ESCALATE IF

1. **A provider in the pool is not authenticated** or the code graph is unavailable and Glob/Grep fallback is insufficient to seed a meaningful frontier.
2. **Executors contradict on a finding's contract** (different signature or reuse verb for the same `unit_id`) and the host cannot reconcile from evidence — surface both sides to the operator.
3. **The loop cannot reach the agreement/relevance gates within the iteration cap** — surface partial findings plus the blocking gate and let the operator decide whether to extend the cap or accept the partial report.

---

## 5. REFERENCES

References are organized into subfolder families (`guides/ protocol/ convergence/ state/`), matching the sibling deep loops.

### Guides

| Document | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| [guides/quick_reference.md](references/guides/quick_reference.md) | One-page operator cheat sheet (commands, params, state files, convergence tree). ALWAYS baseline. |

### Protocol

| Document | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| [protocol/loop_protocol.md](references/protocol/loop_protocol.md) | Iteration lifecycle, parallel dispatch, merge, and host-writes-state invariant |

### Convergence

| Document | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| [convergence/convergence.md](references/convergence/convergence.md) | Stop-contract hub: CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED + why agreement + relevance are guards |
| [convergence/convergence_signals.md](references/convergence/convergence_signals.md) | The 5 signals, composite-score weights, and the threshold reference table |
| [convergence/convergence_recovery.md](references/convergence/convergence_recovery.md) | Blocked-stop recovery foci and stuck-recovery frontier widening |
| [convergence/convergence_graph.md](references/convergence/convergence_graph.md) | The `loop_type='context'` coverage-graph stop path (nodes/edges → signals) |

### State

| Document | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| [state/state_format.md](references/state/state_format.md) | The `context/` packet-file hub: owners, mutability, routing |
| [state/state_jsonl.md](references/state/state_jsonl.md) | `deep-context-state.jsonl` append-only record types |
| [state/state_outputs.md](references/state/state_outputs.md) | Dashboard, iteration files, deltas, and the Context Report outputs |
| [state/state_reducer_registry.md](references/state/state_reducer_registry.md) | `reduce-state.cjs` ownership: registry schema, dedup/agreement, runtime robustness |

### Templates and Assets

| Asset | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| [context_report_template.md](assets/context_report_template.md) | Context Report schema consumed by `/speckit:plan` and `/speckit:implement` |
| [deep_context_config.json](assets/deep_context_config.json) | Run config shape; copied to `{spec_folder}/context/` at init |

### Reference Loading Notes

- Load `references/guides/quick_reference.md` on every invocation (ALWAYS baseline).
- Load the `convergence/` family when tuning stop behavior or diagnosing a blocked stop; the `state/` family when working with the packet, JSONL, or reducer.
- Load `assets/context_report_template.md` when synthesizing or verifying the deliverable.
- Keep Smart Routing as the single routing authority; do not duplicate routing logic here.

---

## 6. SUCCESS CRITERIA

**Task complete when**:
- A Context Report exists at `{spec_folder}/context/context-report.md` with a REUSE catalog whose entries are code-graph-verified (no unlabeled stale refs).
- Convergence stopped via `STOP_ALLOWED` (coverage + relevance + agreement gates passed) or a recorded cap/blocked stop with partial findings and the blocking gate named.
- Per-executor attribution + agreement counts are recorded for every finding in `context/findings-registry.json`.

**Quality gates**:
- `agreementRate >= 0.50` — findings are multi-model-confirmed, not single-seat noise.
- `relevanceFloor >= 0.50` — the loop collected focused context, not tangential files.
- `sliceCoverage >= 0.70` — the defined scope was swept, not partially skimmed.

---

## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS

**Triggers**: pre-planning context gathering; requests matching "gather context", "map the code for X", "what can I reuse for X", `/deep:start-context-loop`.

**Pairs with**:
- `deep-loop-runtime` — coverage-graph (`loop_type='context'`), convergence script, parallel seat dispatch, executor config.
- `sk-prompt-small-model` — per-model prompt framing for the heterogeneous pool.
- `cli-opencode` / `cli-codex` / `cli-claude-code` / `cli-devin` — CLI seat dispatch contracts.
- `system-code-graph` — frontier seeding + reference verification.
- `/speckit:plan` and `/speckit:implement` — downstream consumers of the Context Report.

**Packet layout** (`{spec_folder}/context/`):
- `deep-context-config.json` — run config.
- `deep-context-state.jsonl` — append-only state log.
- `deep-context-strategy.md` — focus and scope notes.
- `iterations/iteration-NNN.md` — per-iteration summaries.
- `findings-registry.json` — agreement-weighted findings (reducer-owned).
- `deep-context-dashboard.md` — auto-generated progress view (reducer-owned).
- `context-report.md` + `context-report.json` — the deliverable.

If the user named a spec folder, or one is derivable from the scope (e.g. a spec-folder path inside the scope text), the packet MUST live at `{spec_folder}/context/`. A standalone run dir is used ONLY when no spec folder is named or derivable — fail-closed, never as a default when a folder is identifiable (see `auto_mode_contract` §1 source 3 + fallback guard). In the standalone case the host hands the report path to `/speckit:plan`.

---

## 8. QUICK REFERENCE

| Item | Value |
|------|-------|
| Command | `/deep:start-context-loop` (`:auto` / `:confirm`) |
| Agent | `@deep-context` (LEAF, read-only analyzer seat) |
| Loop type | `context` in `deep-loop-runtime` coverage-graph + `convergence.cjs` |
| Node kinds | `SLICE, FILE, SYMBOL, PATTERN, REUSE_CANDIDATE, DEPENDENCY, CONSTRAINT, GAP` |
| Convergence signals | `sliceCoverage`, `reuseCatalogCoverage`, `agreementRate` (guard), `relevanceFloor` (guard), `dependencyCompleteness` |
| Packet | `{spec_folder}/context/` (config, state, iterations, `context-report.md`) |
| Default pool | 2 native `@deep-context` seats (native-only), over the shared scope; add `--executor` for CLI/heterogeneous |
| `relevanceGate` | 0.55 (findings below route to `lowConfidence`) |
| `agreementMin` | 2 distinct executors for agreement-eligibility |
| `maxIterations` | 8 |
| `convergenceThreshold` | 0.10 (new agreement-eligible findings per iteration at saturation) |
| `fanout.mode` | `by-model-shared-scope` |
| Stop score weights | reuse-first (0.30 reuseCatalog, 0.25 agreement, 0.20 slice, 0.15 relevance, 0.10 dependency) |
| Runtime robustness | Inherits deep-loop-runtime durability layer: atomic-state, jsonl-repair, post-dispatch-validate, loop-lock (`scripts/loop-lock.cjs`), executor-audit recursion guard |

---

## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES

The router discovers reference, asset, and script docs dynamically. Start with `references/guides/quick_reference.md` (the ALWAYS baseline) and `references/protocol/loop_protocol.md`, then load intent-specific `convergence/` and `state/` resources according to Section 2.

Scripts: `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` — the agreement-weighted context reducer; run it from the repository root with a spec-folder argument.

Related skills: `deep-research` for outward/web investigation (sibling loop whose structure this mirrors), `deep-review` for code audit, `deep-loop-runtime` for shared executor and coverage-graph infrastructure, `sk-prompt-small-model` for per-model prompt framing, and `system-spec-kit` when packet documentation or memory continuity applies.

It runs a heterogeneous pool of executors over the same code scope in parallel and uses cross-executor **agreement** to rank findings by confidence.

27:

30: | Metric | Value |

41: 
42: | Capability | This Skill | Related Skill |

44: | Codebase understanding | Multi-model parallel sweep, agreement-weighted | `@context` handles single-pass read-only lookup |
45: | Web/outward research | Out of scope | `deep-research` owns iterative external investigation |
46: | Code audit and defects | Out of scope | `deep-review` owns iterative defect-finding |

51: 
52: | Feature | What It Does |
53: |---|---|

The Context Report is emitted only when relevance, agreement, and coverage gates all pass.

106: A seat that fails (provider unavailable, timeout) does not block the other seats. The agreement count simply reflects only the seats that returned findings. A pool of 5 with 1 failure still produces 4-executor agreement signals.

108: ### 3.2 FEATURE REFERENCE

111: |---|---|---|---|

119: 
120: ## 4. STRUCTURE
121: 
122: ```text
123: deep-context/
124: +-- SKILL.md                          # Runtime instructions and smart router
125: +-- README.md                         # Human-facing overview (this file)
126: +-- references/
127: |   +-- guides/

129: |   +-- protocol/

131: |   +-- convergence/
