134: |   |   +-- convergence_recovery.md   # Blocked-stop / stuck recovery
135: |   |   +-- convergence_graph.md      # loop_type='context' coverage-graph stop path
136: |   +-- state/
137: |   |   +-- state_format.md           # Packet-file hub (owners, mutability)
138: |   |   +-- state_jsonl.md            # deep-context-state.jsonl record types
139: |   |   +-- state_outputs.md          # Dashboard / Context Report / iteration outputs
140: |   |   +-- state_reducer_registry.md # reduce-state.cjs ownership + robustness
141: +-- assets/
142: |   +-- context_report_template.md   # Context Report schema (REUSE-catalog-first)
143: |   +-- deep_context_config.json     # Run config template; copied to packet at init
144: +-- scripts/
145:     +-- reduce-state.cjs              # Agreement-weighted findings reducer + dashboard
146:     +-- README.md                     # scripts/ folder orientation
147: ```
148: 
149: | Path | Purpose |
150: |---|---|
151: | `SKILL.md` | Runtime routing, rules, and smart router pseudocode |
152: | `references/guides/quick_reference.md` | One-page operator cheat sheet (commands, params, state files, convergence tree) — ALWAYS baseline |
153: | `references/protocol/loop_protocol.md` | Iteration lifecycle, roles, packet layout, merge rules, reliability invariants |
154: | `references/convergence/` | Stop-contract hub + `convergence_signals` (5 signals + weights + thresholds), `convergence_recovery`, `convergence_graph` |
155: | `references/state/` | `state_format` (packet hub), `state_jsonl` (records), `state_outputs` (dashboard/report), `state_reducer_registry` (reducer ownership + robustness) |
156: | `assets/context_report_template.md` | Schema for `context-report.md`; sections ordered by value-per-token |
157: | `assets/deep_context_config.json` | Default run config; populated and written to `{spec_folder}/context/deep-context-config.json` at init |
158: | `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Reads state log + per-seat findings; writes `findings-registry.json` and dashboard |
159: 
160: Runtime packet layout under `{spec_folder}/context/`:
161: 
162: ```text
163: context/
164: +-- deep-context-config.json      # Run config (host-written at init)
165: +-- deep-context-state.jsonl      # Append-only state log
166: +-- deep-context-strategy.md      # Focus and scope notes
167: +-- iterations/
168: |   +-- iteration-001.md
169: |   +-- iteration-002.md
170: +-- seats/
171: |   +-- {label}/iter-NNN/         # Per-seat raw findings (JSON)
172: +-- findings-registry.json        # Agreement-weighted findings (reducer-owned)
173: +-- deep-context-dashboard.md     # Auto-generated progress view (reducer-owned)
174: +-- context-report.md             # The deliverable
175: +-- context-report.json           # Machine-readable deliverable
176: ```
177: 
178: ---
179: 
180: ## 5. CONFIGURATION
181: 
182: | Setting | Default | Purpose |
183: |---|---|---|
184: | `maxIterations` | `8` | Hard iteration ceiling |
185: | `convergenceThreshold` | `0.10` | New agreement-eligible findings per iteration below which saturation is considered |
186: | `stuckThreshold` | `2` | Consecutive no-progress iterations before recovery focus |
187: | `maxToolCallsPerIteration` | `12` | Per-seat tool call budget |
188: | `relevanceGate` | `0.55` | Findings below this go to low-confidence bucket |
189: | `agreementMin` | `2` | Minimum distinct executors for a finding to be agreement-eligible |
190: | `fanout.concurrency` | `4` | Maximum concurrent seats |
191: | `fanout.mode` | `by-model-shared-scope` | All seats sweep the same scope in parallel |
192: 
193: Non-configurable invariants:
194: - The host is the ONLY writer of merged state. Seats are read-only analyzers.
195: - `reduce-state.cjs` owns `findings-registry.json` and `deep-context-dashboard.md`.
196: - Every `file:symbol` in the report must be code-graph-verified before the run completes.
197: 
198: ---
199: 
200: ## 6. USAGE EXAMPLES
201: 
202: **Pre-plan context sweep**
203: 
204: ```text
205: User request: Gather context for the WebSocket reconnection feature before planning
206: Skill routing: FRONTIER_SEEDING + PARALLEL_SWEEP
207: Resources loaded: loop_protocol.md, convergence.md
208: Expected output: Context Report with REUSE catalog, integration points, touch list
209: ```
210: 
211: **Diagnose a blocked stop**
212: 
213: ```text
214: User request: The context loop won't converge — agreementRate is stuck at 0.3
215: Skill routing: CONVERGENCE
216: Resources loaded: convergence.md
217: Expected output: Explanation of which gate is failing and a recovery focus suggestion
218: ```
219: 
220: **Verify the reducer**
221: 
222: ```text
223: User request: Run the reducer against the current context packet
224: Skill routing: AGREEMENT_MERGE
225: Command: node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/reduce-state.cjs .opencode/specs/my-feature
226: Expected output: Updated findings-registry.json and deep-context-dashboard.md
227: ```
228: 
229: ---
230: 
231: ## 7. TROUBLESHOOTING
232: 
233: | What You See | Cause | Fix |
234: |---|---|---|
235: | `agreementRate` stuck below 0.50 | Only one or two seats are returning findings | Check pool authentication; add a native seat to guarantee at least one agreement pair |
236: | `STOP_BLOCKED: sliceCoverage < 0.70` | The frontier has SLICE nodes that no seat has covered | Extend `maxIterations` or narrow the scope to match the iteration budget |
237: | `STOP_BLOCKED: relevanceFloor < 0.50` | Seats are collecting tangential files | Tighten the scope query; increase `relevanceGate` temporarily |
238: | Reducer exits with an error | `deep-context-state.jsonl` has corrupt lines | The reducer auto-repairs a corrupt trailing line via `repairJsonlTail` before reading; if the error persists, inspect the JSONL for mid-file corruption and remove the bad line, then re-run |
239: | Context report missing entries | Below-gate findings ended up in `lowConfidence` | Lower `relevanceGate` slightly, or accept the partial report and note the gaps |
240: | CLI seat times out | Provider is slow or the prompt is too broad | Increase the per-seat timeout; narrow the scope slice; reduce `maxToolCallsPerIteration` |
241: 
242: ---
243: 
244: ## 8. FAQ
245: 
246: **Q: What is the difference between `deep-context` and the `@context` agent?**
247: 
248: A: `@context` is a single-pass read-only lookup — one agent, one request, no iteration. `deep-context` is a convergence-gated multi-iteration loop with a heterogeneous pool. Use `@context` for quick targeted lookups; use `deep-context` when you need a verified, agreement-weighted map of an entire feature's code surface before planning.
249: 
250: **Q: Can I run the loop without a spec folder?**
251: 
252: A: Yes. When no spec folder exists, the host uses a standalone run dir and the report path is handed to `/speckit:plan` at the end. All packet files still live under a `context/` subdirectory of that run dir.
253: 
254: **Q: Why does the report ship pointers instead of source bodies?**
255: 
256: A: Source bodies paste stale code into the report. The consumer (the planner or implementer) reads the actual code at the cited `file:symbol` just-in-time. This prevents context rot and keeps the report token-efficient.
257: 
258: **Q: What happens if a provider in the pool is unavailable?**
259: 
260: A: That seat's findings are absent from the agreement merge. The remaining seats still produce findings. If the absence drops `agreementRate` below the guard, the loop gets a `STOP_BLOCKED` and continues iterating until the gate passes or the cap is hit. Escalate if a required provider cannot be reached at all.
261: 
262: **Q: When should I lower `convergenceThreshold`?**
263: 
264: A: Lower it (e.g. to 0.05) when you want the loop to run more iterations before declaring saturation — useful for broad scope sweeps. Raise it (e.g. to 0.15) when you want to stop faster on a narrow, well-defined feature.
265: 
266: ---
267: 
268: ## 9. RELATED DOCUMENTS
269: 
270: | Document | Purpose |
271: |---|---|
272: | [`SKILL.md`](./SKILL.md) | Runtime instructions, smart router, rules |
273: | [`references/protocol/loop_protocol.md`](./references/protocol/loop_protocol.md) | Iteration lifecycle, roles, packet layout, merge rules |
274: | [`references/convergence/convergence.md`](./references/convergence/convergence.md) | Stop contract, why agreement + relevance are guards (hub) |
275: | [`references/convergence/convergence_signals.md`](./references/convergence/convergence_signals.md) | The 5 signals, composite weights, threshold reference |
276: | [`references/state/`](./references/state/state_format.md) | Packet format, JSONL records, outputs, reducer ownership |
277: | [`references/guides/quick_reference.md`](./references/guides/quick_reference.md) | One-page operator cheat sheet |
278: | [`assets/context_report_template.md`](./assets/context_report_template.md) | Context Report schema and field guide |
279: | [`assets/deep_context_config.json`](./assets/deep_context_config.json) | Default run config template |
280: | [`scripts/README.md`](./scripts/README.md) | scripts/ folder orientation |
281: | [`scripts/reduce-state.cjs`](./scripts/reduce-state.cjs) | Agreement-weighted findings reducer |

(End of file - total 281 lines)

# deep-context

Iterative, multi-model codebase-context-gathering loop that sweeps the existing repository for the code relevant to a feature and synthesizes a reuse-first Context Report before planning or implementation.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

`deep-context` maps existing code so that `/speckit:plan` and `/speckit:implement` start from verified knowledge of what to reuse, where to connect, and which conventions to follow — rather than ad-hoc exploration. It runs a heterogeneous pool of executors over the same code scope in parallel and uses cross-executor **agreement** to rank findings by confidence.

### Usage

Use this README for human orientation: command examples, packet layout, configuration, usage scenarios, troubleshooting, and reference navigation. Use `SKILL.md` for runtime instructions and smart routing.

### Key Statistics

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Version | `1.0.0` |
| Primary command | `/deep:start-context-loop` |
| Runtime modes | `auto`, `confirm` |
| Default executor pool | 2 native `@deep-context` seats (native-only); add `--executor` for CLI/heterogeneous |
| References | 2 (`loop_protocol.md`, `convergence.md`) |
| Assets | 2 (`context_report_template.md`, `deep_context_config.json`) |
| Scripts | 1 (`reduce-state.cjs`) |

### How This Compares

| Capability | This Skill | Related Skill |
|---|---|---|
| Codebase understanding | Multi-model parallel sweep, agreement-weighted | `@context` handles single-pass read-only lookup |
| Web/outward research | Out of scope | `deep-research` owns iterative external investigation |
| Code audit and defects | Out of scope | `deep-review` owns iterative defect-finding |
| Shared runtime | Consumes executor and coverage-graph support | `deep-loop-runtime` owns shared libraries |
| Planning and implementation | Feeds the Context Report to downstream commands | `system-spec-kit` owns spec validation and memory continuity |

### Key Features

| Feature | What It Does |
|---|---|
| Heterogeneous pool | Runs native Claude agents alongside CLI seats (MiMo, gpt, deepseek) in parallel |
| By-model shared scope | All seats sweep the same focus; agreement = multiple models finding the same thing |
| Agreement-weighted findings | A reuse candidate confirmed by 3 of 5 executors outranks a single-seat find |
| Relevance-gated convergence | Below-gate findings go to a low-confidence bucket, not the report |
| Pointer-only report | Ships `file:symbol` references with signatures, not pasted source bodies |
| Contradiction surfacing | Incompatible seat findings are surfaced, never auto-resolved |
| Runtime durability | Crash-safe state writes (atomic temp+fsync+rename), corrupt JSONL tail auto-repaired before each reduce, seat outputs validated before merge (invalid findings surface as `seatValidationWarnings`), single-writer loop-lock (`scripts/loop-lock.cjs`) prevents concurrent session races, CLI seats dispatched with recursion-guard env so no seat can launch a nested loop — durability and validation parity with `deep-research` and `deep-review` |

---

## 2. QUICK START

**Step 1: Invoke the skill.**

Route is automatic from `/deep:start-context-loop`. The skill activates when a request matches context-gathering intent. To load manually:

```bash
# Read runtime instructions
Read(".opencode/skills/deep-context/SKILL.md")
```

**Step 2: Run the primary workflow.**

```bash
/deep:start-context-loop:auto "WebSocket reconnection — map existing transport layer"
/deep:start-context-loop:confirm "auth middleware — what can I reuse before planning"
```

Expected result: a converged Context Report at `{spec_folder}/context/context-report.md` with a REUSE catalog, integration points, touch list, and conventions.

**Step 3: Verify the reducer output.**

```bash
node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/reduce-state.cjs <spec-folder>
```

Expected result: JSON summary with `registryPath`, `dashboardPath`, `iterationsCompleted`, `findings`, `agreementEligible`, and `contradictions`.

**Step 4: Validate the JSON config.**

```bash
node -e "JSON.parse(require('node:fs').readFileSync('.opencode/skills/deep-context/assets/deep_context_config.json','utf8'))" && echo "JSON OK"
```

---

## 3. FEATURES

### 3.1 FEATURE HIGHLIGHTS

The loop runs in two phases: a **parallel sweep phase** where all executor seats analyze the same code scope simultaneously, and a **merge + convergence phase** where the host deduplicates findings by cross-executor agreement and evaluates stop signals. The Context Report is emitted only when relevance, agreement, and coverage gates all pass.

A seat that fails (provider unavailable, timeout) does not block the other seats. The agreement count simply reflects only the seats that returned findings. A pool of 5 with 1 failure still produces 4-executor agreement signals.

### 3.2 FEATURE REFERENCE

| Feature | Inputs | Output | Primary Resource |
|---|---|---|---|
| Frontier seeding | Feature query, code graph | Ranked SLICE nodes | `references/protocol/loop_protocol.md` §3 |
| Parallel sweep | SLICE frontier, executor pool | Per-seat structured findings | `references/protocol/loop_protocol.md` §5 |
| Agreement merge | Raw per-seat findings | `findings-registry.json` | `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` |
| Convergence | Coverage-graph signals | CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED | `references/convergence/convergence.md` |
| Context Report | `findings-registry.json` | `context-report.md` + `.json` | `assets/context_report_template.md` |

---

## 4. STRUCTURE

```text
deep-context/
+-- SKILL.md                          # Runtime instructions and smart router
+-- README.md                         # Human-facing overview (this file)
+-- references/
|   +-- guides/

|   +-- state/

|   |   +-- state_reducer_registry.md # reduce-state.cjs ownership + robustness
+-- assets/
|   +-- context_report_template.md   # Context Report schema (REUSE-catalog-first)
|   +-- deep_context_config.json     # Run config template; copied to packet at init
+-- scripts/
    +-- reduce-state.cjs              # Agreement-weighted findings reducer + dashboard
    +-- README.md                     # scripts/ folder orientation
```

| `assets/context_report_template.md` | Schema for `context-report.md`; sections ordered by value-per-token |
| `assets/deep_context_config.json` | Default run config; populated and written to `{spec_folder}/context/deep-context-config.json` at init |
| `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Reads state log + per-seat findings; writes `findings-registry.json` and dashboard |

Runtime packet layout under `{spec_folder}/context/`:

```text

+-- deep-context-config.json      # Run config (host-written at init)
+-- deep-context-state.jsonl      # Append-only state log
+-- deep-context-strategy.md      # Focus and scope notes
+-- iterations/

+-- findings-registry.json        # Agreement-weighted findings (reducer-owned)
+-- deep-context-dashboard.md     # Auto-generated progress view (reducer-owned)
+-- context-report.md             # The deliverable
+-- context-report.json           # Machine-readable deliverable
```

---

## 5. CONFIGURATION

| Setting | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `maxIterations` | `8` | Hard iteration ceiling |
| `convergenceThreshold` | `0.10` | New agreement-eligible findings per iteration below which saturation is considered |
| `stuckThreshold` | `2` | Consecutive no-progress iterations before recovery focus |
| `maxToolCallsPerIteration` | `12` | Per-seat tool call budget |
| `relevanceGate` | `0.55` | Findings below this go to low-confidence bucket |
| `agreementMin` | `2` | Minimum distinct executors for a finding to be agreement-eligible |
| `fanout.concurrency` | `4` | Maximum concurrent seats |
| `fanout.mode` | `by-model-shared-scope` | All seats sweep the same scope in parallel |

Non-configurable invariants:
- The host is the ONLY writer of merged state. Seats are read-only analyzers.
- `reduce-state.cjs` owns `findings-registry.json` and `deep-context-dashboard.md`.
- Every `file:symbol` in the report must be code-graph-verified before the run completes.

---

## 6. USAGE EXAMPLES

**Pre-plan context sweep**

```text
User request: Gather context for the WebSocket reconnection feature before planning
Skill routing: FRONTIER_SEEDING + PARALLEL_SWEEP
Resources loaded: loop_protocol.md, convergence.md
Expected output: Context Report with REUSE catalog, integration points, touch list
```

**Diagnose a blocked stop**

```text
User request: The context loop won't converge — agreementRate is stuck at 0.3
Skill routing: CONVERGENCE
Resources loaded: convergence.md
Expected output: Explanation of which gate is failing and a recovery focus suggestion
```

**Verify the reducer**

```text
User request: Run the reducer against the current context packet
Skill routing: AGREEMENT_MERGE
Command: node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/reduce-state.cjs .opencode/specs/my-feature
Expected output: Updated findings-registry.json and deep-context-dashboard.md
```

---

## 7. TROUBLESHOOTING

| What You See | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `agreementRate` stuck below 0.50 | Only one or two seats are returning findings | Check pool authentication; add a native seat to guarantee at least one agreement pair |
| `STOP_BLOCKED: sliceCoverage < 0.70` | The frontier has SLICE nodes that no seat has covered | Extend `maxIterations` or narrow the scope to match the iteration budget |
| `STOP_BLOCKED: relevanceFloor < 0.50` | Seats are collecting tangential files | Tighten the scope query; increase `relevanceGate` temporarily |
| Reducer exits with an error | `deep-context-state.jsonl` has corrupt lines | The reducer auto-repairs a corrupt trailing line via `repairJsonlTail` before reading; if the error persists, inspect the JSONL for mid-file corruption and remove the bad line, then re-run |
| Context report missing entries | Below-gate findings ended up in `lowConfidence` | Lower `relevanceGate` slightly, or accept the partial report and note the gaps |
| CLI seat times out | Provider is slow or the prompt is too broad | Increase the per-seat timeout; narrow the scope slice; reduce `maxToolCallsPerIteration` |

---

## 8. FAQ

**Q: What is the difference between `deep-context` and the `@context` agent?**

A: `@context` is a single-pass read-only lookup — one agent, one request, no iteration. `deep-context` is a convergence-gated multi-iteration loop with a heterogeneous pool. Use `@context` for quick targeted lookups; use `deep-context` when you need a verified, agreement-weighted map of an entire feature's code surface before planning.

**Q: Can I run the loop without a spec folder?**

A: Yes. When no spec folder exists, the host uses a standalone run dir and the report path is handed to `/speckit:plan` at the end. All packet files still live under a `context/` subdirectory of that run dir.

**Q: Why does the report ship pointers instead of source bodies?**

A: Source bodies paste stale code into the report. The consumer (the planner or implementer) reads the actual code at the cited `file:symbol` just-in-time. This prevents context rot and keeps the report token-efficient.

**Q: What happens if a provider in the pool is unavailable?**

A: That seat's findings are absent from the agreement merge. The remaining seats still produce findings. If the absence drops `agreementRate` below the guard, the loop gets a `STOP_BLOCKED` and continues iterating until the gate passes or the cap is hit. Escalate if a required provider cannot be reached at all.

**Q: When should I lower `convergenceThreshold`?**

A: Lower it (e.g. to 0.05) when you want the loop to run more iterations before declaring saturation — useful for broad scope sweeps. Raise it (e.g. to 0.15) when you want to stop faster on a narrow, well-defined feature.

---

## 9. RELATED DOCUMENTS

| Document | Purpose |
|---|---|
| [`SKILL.md`](./SKILL.md) | Runtime instructions, smart router, rules |
| [`references/protocol/loop_protocol.md`](./references/protocol/loop_protocol.md) | Iteration lifecycle, roles, packet layout, merge rules |
| [`references/convergence/convergence.md`](./references/convergence/convergence.md) | Stop contract, why agreement + relevance are guards (hub) |
| [`references/convergence/convergence_signals.md`](./references/convergence/convergence_signals.md) | The 5 signals, composite weights, threshold reference |
| [`references/state/`](./references/state/state_format.md) | Packet format, JSONL records, outputs, reducer ownership |
| [`references/guides/quick_reference.md`](./references/guides/quick_reference.md) | One-page operator cheat sheet |
| [`assets/context_report_template.md`](./assets/context_report_template.md) | Context Report schema and field guide |
| [`assets/deep_context_config.json`](./assets/deep_context_config.json) | Default run config template |
| [`scripts/README.md`](./scripts/README.md) | scripts/ folder orientation |
| [`scripts/reduce-state.cjs`](./scripts/reduce-state.cjs) | Agreement-weighted findings reducer |

1: ---

8: # Deep Loop Runtime
9: 
10: Shared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.
11: 
12: ---
13: 
14: ## 1. WHEN TO USE
15: 
16: This skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.
17: 
18: Use it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:
19: 
20: - executor configuration and audit metadata
21: - prompt-pack rendering
22: - post-dispatch validation
23: - atomic state files and JSONL repair
24: - loop locking and permissions gates
25: - Bayesian scoring and fallback routing
26: - coverage-graph schema, query, and signal interpretation
27: 
28: ### Activation Triggers
29: 
30: This skill is activated when:
31: 
32: - A deep-review or deep-research workflow YAML invokes `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/<name>.cjs`
33: - A test file imports `from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/...'`
34: - An operator queries / mutates the coverage graph via the runtime scripts
35: - A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing
36: 
37: ### When NOT to Use
38: 
39: Do not invoke this skill when:
40: 
41: - A user requests an interactive command (use `/deep:start-review-loop` or `/deep:start-research-loop` instead — those orchestrate this runtime)
42: - A package outside deep-review / deep-research needs deep-loop semantics (out of supported scope; open a packet to extend ownership)
43: - An MCP tool surface is needed (this skill explicitly has none — the isolation ADR removed the deep-loop MCP tools)
44: 
45: ---
46: 
47: ## 2. SMART ROUTING
48: 
49: This skill has no direct LLM-facing keyword triggers — it is invoked by workflow YAMLs (via `bash:` script calls) and by test imports. The router below documents which sub-domain to load for a given consumer query.
50: 
51: ### Primary Detection Signal
52: 
53: ```bash
54: request_text="$(printf '%s' "$USER_REQUEST" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')"
55: case "$request_text" in
56:   *"deep-loop runtime"*|*"deep-loop-runtime"*|*"executor config"*|*"prompt pack"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;
57:   *"coverage graph"*|*"deep_loop_graph"*|*"bayesian scorer"*|*"loop lock"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;
58:   *"fallback router"*|*"post-dispatch validate"*|*"jsonl repair"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;
59:   *) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=0 ;;
60: esac
61: ```
62: 
63: ### Phase Detection
64: 
65: ```text
66: TASK CONTEXT
67:     |
68:     +- STEP 0: Detect runtime-call intent vs. consumer-orchestration intent
69:     +- STEP 1: If consumer (workflow YAML), invoke the relevant scripts/*.cjs
70:     +- STEP 2: If contributor (modifying lib code), load matching lib/ subtree
71:     +- STEP 3: If test author, load tests/ + lib/ pair
72: ```
73: 
74: ### Resource Domains
75: 
76: ```text
77: lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)
78: lib/coverage-graph/*.ts       # 3 schema + query + signals modules
79: scripts/*.cjs                 # 4 entry points (convergence, upsert, query, status)
80: database/*.sqlite              # runtime-owned SQLite (deep-loop-graph.sqlite)
81: tests/{unit,integration,lifecycle}/  # 21+ vitest files split by responsibility
82: ```
83: 
84: ### Resource Loading Levels
85: 
86: | Tier | When loaded | Purpose |
87: |------|-------------|---------|
88: | ALWAYS | SKILL.md frontmatter | description, allowed-tools, version |
89: | CONDITIONAL | When a runtime call dispatches | `lib/` modules required by the called function |
90: | ON_DEMAND | When a test fails or a contributor extends | `tests/` + matching `lib/` sub-tree |
91: 
92: ### Smart Router Pseudocode
93: 
94: ```text
95: IF request mentions a specific lib name (e.g. "bayesian-scorer", "prompt-pack"):
96:   → Load lib/deep-loop/<name>.ts + matching tests/unit/<name>.vitest.ts
97: ELIF request mentions coverage-graph / convergence / signals:
98:   → Load lib/coverage-graph/*.ts + tests/integration/review-depth-*.vitest.ts
99: ELIF request mentions a script (convergence.cjs, status.cjs, etc.):
100:   → Load scripts/<name>.cjs + tests/integration/<name>-script.vitest.ts + lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts
101: ELSE:
102:   → Defer to consumer skill (deep-review / deep-research)
103: ```
104: 
105: ---
106: 
107: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS
108: 
109: ### Runtime Architecture
110: 
111: The runtime exposes deep-loop primitives to consumer workflows through two paths: **direct `.cjs` script invocation** (replacing the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools) and **TypeScript library imports** (from workflow scripts and tests).
112: 
113: ```text
114: [deep-review/deep-research workflow YAML]
115:             │
116:             ├─ bash: node .../scripts/<name>.cjs   (coverage-graph mutations)
117:             │       └─→ lib/coverage-graph/*.ts → database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite
118:             │
119:             └─ ts-import: lib/deep-loop/<lib>.ts   (runtime primitives)
120:                     └─→ atomic-state, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, etc.
121: ```
122: 
123: ### Library Structure
124: 
125: **`lib/deep-loop/` (10 modules):**
126: - `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings
127: - `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)
128: - `prompt-pack.ts` — renders the iteration prompt template
129: - `post-dispatch-validate.ts` — validates iteration outputs (markdown + JSONL + delta)
130: - `atomic-state.ts` — atomic state-log writes
131: - `jsonl-repair.ts` — recovers corrupt JSONL state lines
132: - `loop-lock.ts` — single-writer locking
133: - `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks
134: - `bayesian-scorer.ts` — convergence scoring
135: - `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix
136: 
137: **`lib/coverage-graph/` (3 modules):**
138: - `coverage-graph-db.ts` — SQLite schema + node-kind allow-list + connection lifecycle
139: - `coverage-graph-query.ts` — query builders
140: - `coverage-graph-signals.ts` — convergence signal extraction
141: 
142: ### Council Primitives
143: 
144: The Runtime Boundary Decision (ADR-001) extends `deep-loop-runtime/lib/` with council-compatible infrastructure primitives while keeping operator-facing and domain workflow semantics in `deep-ai-council`. These primitives are consumed by downstream deep-ai-council orchestration (per-topic multi-round, session-findings registry, command and skill wiring, parity and cost docs) for per-topic orchestration, multi-topic session state, command wiring, parity tests and docs.
145: 
146: **`lib/council/` (5 modules):**
147: - `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.
148: - `round-state-jsonl.cjs` — appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard, repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append, fsyncs writes, and exposes round-state readers for resume.
149: - `adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs` — scores Round-N -> Round-N+1 adjudicator verdict deltas using ADR-003 weights for option change, confidence delta, material-risk Jaccard delta, axis flip rate, and blocking-disagreement delta.
150: - `cost-guards.cjs` — normalizes and enforces ADR-004 defaults for `max_rounds_per_topic`, `max_topics_per_session`, `saturation_threshold`, and `seats_per_round`, including upper-bound seat-output calculation.
151: - `session-state-hierarchy.cjs` — creates and validates the ADR-002 session -> topic -> round state shape, including stable `topic-NNN-slug` and `round-NNN` ids.
152: 
153: The existing `lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts`, and `lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` remain the sibling runtime contracts. Council modules mirror those durability semantics in a council-scoped CJS surface so downstream `deep-ai-council` phases can consume them without modifying deep-review or deep-research behavior.
154: 
155: ### Script Entry Points
156: 
157: Four `.cjs` scripts replace the 4 deleted MCP tools, each honoring the same JSON-in / JSON-out contract documented in ADR-001 (the script interface contract):
158: 
159: | Script | Replaces | Purpose | Exit codes |
160: |--------|----------|---------|------------|
161: | `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes typed CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED decisions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |
162: | `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores nodes + edges from iteration `graphEvents` | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |
163: | `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims, contradictions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |
164: | `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Session-scoped health report | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |
165: | `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |
166: | `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |
167: | `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |
168: | `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |
169: 
170: **Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.
171: 
172: Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.
173: 
174: ### Test Organization
175: 
176: ```text
177: tests/
178: ├── unit/         # 13 files — per-module unit tests (atomic-state, executor-config, etc.)
179: ├── integration/  # 7 files — script invocations + review-depth fixtures
180: ├── lifecycle/    # 1 file — db-open-close pattern verification
181: └── _helpers/     # 1 file — spawn-cjs helper for direct-invocation tests
182: ```
183: 
184: The system-spec-kit `mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` so a single vitest run discovers both surfaces.
185: 
186: ---
187: 
188: ## 4. RULES
189: 
190: ### ALWAYS
191: 
192: - **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)
193: - **ALWAYS** emit JSON-only output to stdout from `scripts/*.cjs` (workflow YAML parses stdout for output bindings)
194: - **ALWAYS** include `'use strict';` on line 2 of every `.cjs` script (per sk-code OPENCODE JS style)
195: - **ALWAYS** include a `MODULE: <name>` header comment in every `lib/**/*.ts` file (per sk-code OPENCODE TS-MODULE-HEADER convention)
196: - **ALWAYS** preserve atomic-state semantics when mutating JSONL state — never partial-write
197: - **ALWAYS** acquire `loop-lock` before any state-log mutation
198: 
199: ### NEVER
200: 
201: - **NEVER** register MCP tools from this skill — the isolation ADR explicitly removed the MCP surface; reintroducing tools defeats the FULL_ISOLATE direction
202: - **NEVER** open the SQLite DB outside `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` — single owner of the connection
203: - **NEVER** import from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/` or `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/` — those locations are empty after the runtime moved here; import from this skill instead
204: - **NEVER** write to `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` without holding the `loop-lock`
205: - **NEVER** swallow exceptions in script entry points — propagate to the exit-code matrix
206: 
207: ### ESCALATE IF
208: 
209: - **ESCALATE** to the user if a runtime call requires a new MCP tool — the architectural direction prohibits MCP additions; the user must decide on policy override
210: - **ESCALATE** to a new ownership ADR if a new consumer skill (beyond deep-review and deep-research) needs deep-loop runtime — extension requires a new ownership ADR
211: - **ESCALATE** if the SQLite schema requires a backward-incompatible change — coordinate with all consumer workflows + add migration to `coverage-graph-db.ts`
212: - **ESCALATE** if a script's stdout JSON contract changes — every workflow YAML output binding depends on the existing shape
213: 
214: ---
215: 
216: ## 5. SCOPE
217: 
218: In scope:
219: 
220: - runtime libraries under `lib/deep-loop/`
221: - coverage-graph runtime libraries under `lib/coverage-graph/`
222: - `.cjs` script entry points under `scripts/`
223: - runtime-owned SQLite storage under `database/`
224: - runtime tests under `tests/`
225: 
226: Out of scope:
227: 
228: - MCP tool registration
229: - MCP handler ownership
230: - DB lifecycle inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`
231: - direct user command routing
232: 
233: ---
234: 
235: ## 6. ARCHITECTURE
236: 
237: - `lib/deep-loop/` holds shared deep-loop runtime libraries.
238: - `lib/coverage-graph/` holds coverage-graph schema, query, and signal helpers.
239: - `scripts/` holds `.cjs` entry points used by workflow YAML and collateral commands.
240: - `database/` owns relocated runtime SQLite artifacts.
241: - `tests/` holds runtime-owned tests split from the MCP server surface.
242: 
243: Shipped layout:
244: 
245: | Path | Populated By | Purpose |
246: |------|--------------|---------|
247: | `lib/deep-loop/` | 10 files | Shared executor config, audit, prompt-pack, validation, state, repair, locking, permissions, scoring, and fallback routing. |
248: | `lib/coverage-graph/` | 3 files | Coverage-graph schema owner, query helpers, and signal interpretation. |
249: | `scripts/` | 8 `.cjs` files | `status`/`query`/`upsert`/`convergence` shims replacing the removed deep-loop MCP tools, plus the fan-out (multi-executor) entry points `fanout-run`/`fanout-pool`/`fanout-salvage`/`fanout-merge`. |
250: | `database/` | SQLite | Runtime-owned `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |
251: | `tests/` | Unit, integration, lifecycle | Runtime-owned tests discovered by the system-spec-kit MCP server Vitest config. |
252: 
253: ### Per-runtime agent mirrors (consumer convention)
254: 
255: This runtime is MCP-free and owns no agents, but every deep loop that consumes it ships a **native agent** that the loop dispatches **by name** (`agent: <name>` in the loop YAML), which each host runtime resolves from its OWN `agents/` dir. Each such agent — `deep-context`, `deep-research`, `deep-review`, `deep-improvement`, `ai-council` — is one canonical source plus two runtime mirrors carrying the same body:
256: 
257: - `.opencode/agents/<name>.md` — **canonical** (OpenCode frontmatter: `mode: subagent` + `permission:` block)
258: - `.claude/agents/<name>.md` — Claude mirror (`tools:` allow-list frontmatter)
259: - `.codex/agents/<name>.toml` — Codex mirror (`developer_instructions = '''…'''` + `# Converted from:` header + `sandbox_mode`)
260: 
261: The loop commands and YAML are **shared** across runtimes (the `.claude/`/`.codex/` `commands`/`prompts`/`skills` dirs are symlinks to `.opencode/`), so they reference canonical `.opencode/` paths and dispatch by name on purpose — do NOT fork them per runtime. A deep-loop agent present in `.opencode/agents/` but missing its `.claude/` or `.codex/` mirror silently fails to dispatch in that runtime (CLI seats still run; only the native seats drop). **When adding or renaming a deep-loop native agent, create/update all three files and verify three-way parity** (e.g. compare `ls .opencode/agents .claude/agents .codex/agents`).
262: 
263: ---
264: 
265: ## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS
266: 
267: - **ADR-001 (AI Council SPLIT ruling)**: original AI Council SPLIT ruling. Superseded by user directive.
268: - **Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP)**: Moves deep-loop runtime, coverage-graph schema/query/signals, script entry points, storage, and tests into this peer skill while removing the deep-loop MCP tool surface.
269: - **Deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML** call this runtime through script entry points after the runtime cutover.
270: - **system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts** includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` for cross-package test discovery.
271: - **deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs** depends on the moved coverage-graph runtime via lib imports.
272: 
273: ---
274: 
275: ## 8. SUCCESS CRITERIA
276: 
277: The runtime is healthy when:
278: 
279: - Every `scripts/` entry point (`status`, `query`, `upsert`, `convergence`) returns valid JSON on its documented stdout contract
280: - Consumer workflows resolve their `lib/` imports without path errors
281: - The runtime-owned SQLite under `database/` is present and its coverage-graph schema matches `coverage-graph-db.ts`
282: - Runtime tests under `tests/` pass through the system-spec-kit Vitest discovery glob
283: 
284: ---
285: 
286: ## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES
287: 
288: - ADR-001 (script interface contract): documents the script-shim and DB-relocation contract.
289: - Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP): documents the MCP tool surface removal rationale.
290: - Decision rationale lives in this skill's changelog and originating decision records.
291: 
292: Related resources:
293: 
294: - Consumer skills: the `deep-review` and `deep-research` workflows call this runtime through the script entry points
295: - `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` discovers this skill's `tests/`
296: - Per-release detail and rationale: this skill's `changelog/` directory

(End of file - total 296 lines)
</content>

Shared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.

---

## 1. WHEN TO USE

This skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.

Use it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:

- executor configuration and audit metadata
- prompt-pack rendering
- post-dispatch validation
- atomic state files and JSONL repair
- loop locking and permissions gates
- Bayesian scoring and fallback routing
- coverage-graph schema, query, and signal interpretation

### Activation Triggers

This skill is activated when:

- A deep-review or deep-research workflow YAML invokes `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/<name>.cjs`
- A test file imports `from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/...'`
- An operator queries / mutates the coverage graph via the runtime scripts
- A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing

### When NOT to Use

Do not invoke this skill when:

- A user requests an interactive command (use `/deep:start-review-loop` or `/deep:start-research-loop` instead — those orchestrate this runtime)
- A package outside deep-review / deep-research needs deep-loop semantics (out of supported scope; open a packet to extend ownership)
- An MCP tool surface is needed (this skill explicitly has none — the isolation ADR removed the deep-loop MCP tools)

---

## 2. SMART ROUTING

This skill has no direct LLM-facing keyword triggers — it is invoked by workflow YAMLs (via `bash:` script calls) and by test imports. The router below documents which sub-domain to load for a given consumer query.

### Primary Detection Signal

```bash
request_text="$(printf '%s' "$USER_REQUEST" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')"
case "$request_text" in
  *"deep-loop runtime"*|*"deep-loop-runtime"*|*"executor config"*|*"prompt pack"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;
  *"coverage graph"*|*"deep_loop_graph"*|*"bayesian scorer"*|*"loop lock"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;
  *"fallback router"*|*"post-dispatch validate"*|*"jsonl repair"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;
  *) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=0 ;;
esac
```

### Phase Detection

```text
TASK CONTEXT
    |
    +- STEP 0: Detect runtime-call intent vs. consumer-orchestration intent
    +- STEP 1: If consumer (workflow YAML), invoke the relevant scripts/*.cjs
    +- STEP 2: If contributor (modifying lib code), load matching lib/ subtree
    +- STEP 3: If test author, load tests/ + lib/ pair
```

### Resource Domains

```text
lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)
lib/coverage-graph/*.ts       # 3 schema + query + signals modules
scripts/*.cjs                 # 4 entry points (convergence, upsert, query, status)
database/*.sqlite              # runtime-owned SQLite (deep-loop-graph.sqlite)
tests/{unit,integration,lifecycle}/  # 21+ vitest files split by responsibility
```

### Resource Loading Levels

| Tier | When loaded | Purpose |
|------|-------------|---------|
| ALWAYS | SKILL.md frontmatter | description, allowed-tools, version |
| CONDITIONAL | When a runtime call dispatches | `lib/` modules required by the called function |
| ON_DEMAND | When a test fails or a contributor extends | `tests/` + matching `lib/` sub-tree |

### Smart Router Pseudocode

```text
IF request mentions a specific lib name (e.g. "bayesian-scorer", "prompt-pack"):
  → Load lib/deep-loop/<name>.ts + matching tests/unit/<name>.vitest.ts
ELIF request mentions coverage-graph / convergence / signals:
  → Load lib/coverage-graph/*.ts + tests/integration/review-depth-*.vitest.ts
ELIF request mentions a script (convergence.cjs, status.cjs, etc.):
  → Load scripts/<name>.cjs + tests/integration/<name>-script.vitest.ts + lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts
ELSE:
  → Defer to consumer skill (deep-review / deep-research)
```

---

## 3. HOW IT WORKS

### Runtime Architecture

The runtime exposes deep-loop primitives to consumer workflows through two paths: **direct `.cjs` script invocation** (replacing the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools) and **TypeScript library imports** (from workflow scripts and tests).

```text
[deep-review/deep-research workflow YAML]
            │
            ├─ bash: node .../scripts/<name>.cjs   (coverage-graph mutations)
            │       └─→ lib/coverage-graph/*.ts → database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite
            │
            └─ ts-import: lib/deep-loop/<lib>.ts   (runtime primitives)
                    └─→ atomic-state, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, etc.
```

### Library Structure

**`lib/deep-loop/` (10 modules):**
- `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings
- `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)
- `prompt-pack.ts` — renders the iteration prompt template
- `post-dispatch-validate.ts` — validates iteration outputs (markdown + JSONL + delta)
- `atomic-state.ts` — atomic state-log writes
- `jsonl-repair.ts` — recovers corrupt JSONL state lines
- `loop-lock.ts` — single-writer locking
- `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks
- `bayesian-scorer.ts` — convergence scoring
- `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix

**`lib/coverage-graph/` (3 modules):**
- `coverage-graph-db.ts` — SQLite schema + node-kind allow-list + connection lifecycle
- `coverage-graph-query.ts` — query builders
- `coverage-graph-signals.ts` — convergence signal extraction

### Council Primitives

The Runtime Boundary Decision (ADR-001) extends `deep-loop-runtime/lib/` with council-compatible infrastructure primitives while keeping operator-facing and domain workflow semantics in `deep-ai-council`. These primitives are consumed by downstream deep-ai-council orchestration (per-topic multi-round, session-findings registry, command and skill wiring, parity and cost docs) for per-topic orchestration, multi-topic session state, command wiring, parity tests and docs.

**`lib/council/` (5 modules):**
- `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.
- `round-state-jsonl.cjs` — appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard, repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append, fsyncs writes, and exposes round-state readers for resume.
- `adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs` — scores Round-N -> Round-N+1 adjudicator verdict deltas using ADR-003 weights for option change, confidence delta, material-risk Jaccard delta, axis flip rate, and blocking-disagreement delta.
- `cost-guards.cjs` — normalizes and enforces ADR-004 defaults for `max_rounds_per_topic`, `max_topics_per_session`, `saturation_threshold`, and `seats_per_round`, including upper-bound seat-output calculation.
- `session-state-hierarchy.cjs` — creates and validates the ADR-002 session -> topic -> round state shape, including stable `topic-NNN-slug` and `round-NNN` ids.

The existing `lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts`, and `lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` remain the sibling runtime contracts. Council modules mirror those durability semantics in a council-scoped CJS surface so downstream `deep-ai-council` phases can consume them without modifying deep-review or deep-research behavior.

### Script Entry Points

Four `.cjs` scripts replace the 4 deleted MCP tools, each honoring the same JSON-in / JSON-out contract documented in ADR-001 (the script interface contract):

| Script | Replaces | Purpose | Exit codes |
|--------|----------|---------|------------|
| `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes typed CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED decisions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |
| `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores nodes + edges from iteration `graphEvents` | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |
| `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims, contradictions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |
| `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Session-scoped health report | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |
| `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |
| `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |
| `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |
| `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |

**Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.

Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.

### Test Organization

```text
tests/
├── unit/         # 13 files — per-module unit tests (atomic-state, executor-config, etc.)
├── integration/  # 7 files — script invocations + review-depth fixtures
├── lifecycle/    # 1 file — db-open-close pattern verification
└── _helpers/     # 1 file — spawn-cjs helper for direct-invocation tests
```

The system-spec-kit `mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` so a single vitest run discovers both surfaces.

---

## 4. RULES

### ALWAYS

- **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)
- **ALWAYS** emit JSON-only output to stdout from `scripts/*.cjs` (workflow YAML parses stdout for output bindings)
- **ALWAYS** include `'use strict';` on line 2 of every `.cjs` script (per sk-code OPENCODE JS style)
- **ALWAYS** include a `MODULE: <name>` header comment in every `lib/**/*.ts` file (per sk-code OPENCODE TS-MODULE-HEADER convention)
- **ALWAYS** preserve atomic-state semantics when mutating JSONL state — never partial-write
- **ALWAYS** acquire `loop-lock` before any state-log mutation

### NEVER

- **NEVER** register MCP tools from this skill — the isolation ADR explicitly removed the MCP surface; reintroducing tools defeats the FULL_ISOLATE direction
- **NEVER** open the SQLite DB outside `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` — single owner of the connection
- **NEVER** import from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/` or `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/` — those locations are empty after the runtime moved here; import from this skill instead
- **NEVER** write to `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` without holding the `loop-lock`
- **NEVER** swallow exceptions in script entry points — propagate to the exit-code matrix

### ESCALATE IF

- **ESCALATE** to the user if a runtime call requires a new MCP tool — the architectural direction prohibits MCP additions; the user must decide on policy override
- **ESCALATE** to a new ownership ADR if a new consumer skill (beyond deep-review and deep-research) needs deep-loop runtime — extension requires a new ownership ADR
- **ESCALATE** if the SQLite schema requires a backward-incompatible change — coordinate with all consumer workflows + add migration to `coverage-graph-db.ts`
- **ESCALATE** if a script's stdout JSON contract changes — every workflow YAML output binding depends on the existing shape

---

## 5. SCOPE

In scope:

- runtime libraries under `lib/deep-loop/`
- coverage-graph runtime libraries under `lib/coverage-graph/`
- `.cjs` script entry points under `scripts/`
- runtime-owned SQLite storage under `database/`
- runtime tests under `tests/`

Out of scope:

- MCP tool registration
- MCP handler ownership
- DB lifecycle inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`
- direct user command routing

---

## 6. ARCHITECTURE

- `lib/deep-loop/` holds shared deep-loop runtime libraries.
- `lib/coverage-graph/` holds coverage-graph schema, query, and signal helpers.
- `scripts/` holds `.cjs` entry points used by workflow YAML and collateral commands.
- `database/` owns relocated runtime SQLite artifacts.
- `tests/` holds runtime-owned tests split from the MCP server surface.

Shipped layout:

| Path | Populated By | Purpose |
|------|--------------|---------|
| `lib/deep-loop/` | 10 files | Shared executor config, audit, prompt-pack, validation, state, repair, locking, permissions, scoring, and fallback routing. |
| `lib/coverage-graph/` | 3 files | Coverage-graph schema owner, query helpers, and signal interpretation. |
| `scripts/` | 8 `.cjs` files | `status`/`query`/`upsert`/`convergence` shims replacing the removed deep-loop MCP tools, plus the fan-out (multi-executor) entry points `fanout-run`/`fanout-pool`/`fanout-salvage`/`fanout-merge`. |
| `database/` | SQLite | Runtime-owned `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |
| `tests/` | Unit, integration, lifecycle | Runtime-owned tests discovered by the system-spec-kit MCP server Vitest config. |

### Per-runtime agent mirrors (consumer convention)

This runtime is MCP-free and owns no agents, but every deep loop that consumes it ships a **native agent** that the loop dispatches **by name** (`agent: <name>` in the loop YAML), which each host runtime resolves from its OWN `agents/` dir. Each such agent — `deep-context`, `deep-research`, `deep-review`, `deep-improvement`, `ai-council` — is one canonical source plus two runtime mirrors carrying the same body:

- `.opencode/agents/<name>.md` — **canonical** (OpenCode frontmatter: `mode: subagent` + `permission:` block)
- `.claude/agents/<name>.md` — Claude mirror (`tools:` allow-list frontmatter)
- `.codex/agents/<name>.toml` — Codex mirror (`developer_instructions = '''…'''` + `# Converted from:` header + `sandbox_mode`)

The loop commands and YAML are **shared** across runtimes (the `.claude/`/`.codex/` `commands`/`prompts`/`skills` dirs are symlinks to `.opencode/`), so they reference canonical `.opencode/` paths and dispatch by name on purpose — do NOT fork them per runtime. A deep-loop agent present in `.opencode/agents/` but missing its `.claude/` or `.codex/` mirror silently fails to dispatch in that runtime (CLI seats still run; only the native seats drop). **When adding or renaming a deep-loop native agent, create/update all three files and verify three-way parity** (e.g. compare `ls .opencode/agents .claude/agents .codex/agents`).

---

## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS

- **ADR-001 (AI Council SPLIT ruling)**: original AI Council SPLIT ruling. Superseded by user directive.
- **Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP)**: Moves deep-loop runtime, coverage-graph schema/query/signals, script entry points, storage, and tests into this peer skill while removing the deep-loop MCP tool surface.
- **Deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML** call this runtime through script entry points after the runtime cutover.
- **system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts** includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` for cross-package test discovery.
- **deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs** depends on the moved coverage-graph runtime via lib imports.

---

## 8. SUCCESS CRITERIA

The runtime is healthy when:

- Every `scripts/` entry point (`status`, `query`, `upsert`, `convergence`) returns valid JSON on its documented stdout contract
- Consumer workflows resolve their `lib/` imports without path errors
- The runtime-owned SQLite under `database/` is present and its coverage-graph schema matches `coverage-graph-db.ts`
- Runtime tests under `tests/` pass through the system-spec-kit Vitest discovery glob

---

## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES

- ADR-001 (script interface contract): documents the script-shim and DB-relocation contract.
- Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP): documents the MCP tool surface removal rationale.
- Decision rationale lives in this skill's changelog and originating decision records.

Related resources:

- Consumer skills: the `deep-review` and `deep-research` workflows call this runtime through the script entry points
- `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` discovers this skill's `tests/`
- Per-release detail and rationale: this skill's `changelog/` directory

509:           path: "{state_paths.delta_pattern}"
510:           note: "Must contain at least one record whose type === 'iteration' (host-written) so state can be rehydrated after a partial run."
511: 
512:       step_graph_upsert:
513:         action: "Persist this iteration's coverage-graph nodes/edges (loop_type=context)"
514:         if_graph_events_present:
515:           bash: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/upsert.cjs --spec-folder \"{spec_folder}\" --loop-type \"context\" --session-id \"{config.lineage.sessionId}\" --nodes '{graph_nodes_json}' --edges '{graph_edges_json}'"
516:           note: "Node kinds + relations for loop_type=context are defined in coverage-graph-db.ts (VALID_KINDS.context / VALID_RELATIONS.context). Skip silently when there are no graph events."
517:         if_graph_events_missing:
518:           proceed: true
519: 
520:       step_update_registry:
521:         action: "Refresh the merged findings registry + dashboard deterministically from iteration state"
522:         bash: "node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}"
523:         note: "The reducer is the writer (parity with deep-research/deep-review): re-reads deep-context-state.jsonl + every seat finding, repairs a crash-truncated JSONL tail before read, validates each seat finding before merge, and writes findings-registry.json + dashboard via atomic temp+rename. This is the step that auto-utilizes the runtime robustness; it surfaces stateLogRepair + seatValidationWarnings in its JSON result."
524:         outputs:
525:           - registry_path: "{state_paths.registry}"
526:           - dashboard_path: "{state_paths.dashboard}"
527: 
528:       step_graph_convergence:
529:         action: "Evaluate coverage-graph convergence and persist the decision snapshot"
530:         bash: 'node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/convergence.cjs --spec-folder "{spec_folder}" --loop-type "context" --session-id "{config.lineage.sessionId}" --iteration {current_iteration}'
531:         note: "convergence.cjs#evaluateContext returns CONTINUE | STOP_ALLOWED | STOP_BLOCKED from the context signals (sliceCoverage, reuseCatalogCoverage, agreementRate, relevanceFloor, dependencyCompleteness) plus contradiction warnings. See convergence.md §3."
532:         outputs:
533:           - graph_decision: "CONTINUE | STOP_ALLOWED | STOP_BLOCKED, else null when unavailable"
534:           - graph_decision_json: "JSON-encoded graph decision string, else null"
535:           - graph_signals_json: "Tool signals object, else {}"
536:           - graph_blockers_json: "Tool blockers array, else []"
537:           - graph_convergence_score: "signals.blendedScore when present, else best available graph score, else 0"
538:         append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"graph_convergence","mode":"context","run":{current_iteration},"decision":{graph_decision_json},"signals":{graph_signals_json},"blockers":{graph_blockers_json},"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
539: 
540:       step_check_convergence:
541:         action: "Combine the host per-iteration saturation check with the graph context decision"
542:         algorithm: |
543:           1. if iteration_count >= max_iterations: STOP (maxIterationsReached). Terminal cap; record
544:              failed guards/blockers as synthesis evidence instead of continuing.
545:           2. if stuck_count >= config.stuckThreshold: STUCK_RECOVERY.
546:           3. HOST SATURATION CHECK (per-iteration new agreement-eligible findings):
547:                new_ratio = new_agreement_eligible_count / max(1, merged_findings_count)
548:                if new_ratio < convergence_threshold: low_progress_streak += 1, else low_progress_streak = 0
549:                host_saturated = low_progress_streak >= 2   (K=2 successive low-progress iterations)
550:           4. COMBINED STOP RULE (agreement + relevance are blocking guards, enforced by the graph):
551:                - STOP only if host_saturated AND graph_decision == "STOP_ALLOWED" (or absent).
552:                - if host_saturated AND graph_decision == "STOP_BLOCKED":
553:                    decision = "BLOCKED"; reason = "blockedStop"
554:                    blocked_by = graph_blockers (e.g. sliceCoverage<0.70, relevanceFloor<0.50, agreementRate<0.50)
555:                    recovery_strategy = first blocker message, else "Sweep uncovered slices; raise agreement before stopping."
556:                - else if host_saturated AND STOP_ALLOWED: decision = "STOP"; reason = "converged"
557:                - else: decision = "CONTINUE"
558:           See convergence.md for the full signal table, composite weights, and stop contract.
559:         outputs:
560:           - decision: "STOP | BLOCKED | STUCK_RECOVERY | CONTINUE"
561:           - reason: "converged | maxIterationsReached | blockedStop | stuckRecovery | error"
562:           - blocked_by_json: "JSON array of graph blocker names when decision == BLOCKED"
563:           - blocked_by_csv: "Comma-separated graph blocker names when decision == BLOCKED"
564:           - recovery_strategy: "One-line hint for the next focus when BLOCKED or STUCK_RECOVERY"
565: 
566:       step_emit_blocked_stop:
567:         action: "Persist a first-class blocked_stop event when convergence returns blocked, then continue with a recovery focus"
568:         if_blocked:
569:           append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"blocked_stop","mode":"context","run":{current_iteration},"blockedBy":{blocked_by_json},"signals":{graph_signals_json},"recoveryStrategy":"{recovery_strategy}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
570:           log: "Graph convergence blocked STOP. Failed blockers: {blocked_by_csv}. Recovery: {recovery_strategy}"
571:           set:
572:             decision: "CONTINUE"
573:             reason: "blockedStop"
574:             next_focus: "RECOVERY: {recovery_strategy}. Prioritize uncovered slices: {uncovered_slices}"
575: 
576:       step_handle_convergence:
577:         action: "Act on convergence decision"
578:         if_stop:
579:           log: "Loop stopping. Reason: {reason}"
580:           skip_to: phase_synthesis
581:         if_stuck_recovery:
582:           append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"stuckRecovery","mode":"context","run":{current_iteration},"stopReason":"stuckRecovery","fromIteration":{current_iteration},"outcome":"recovered","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
583:           log: "Stuck detected ({stuck_count} consecutive low-progress sweeps). Widening focus."
584:           set: { next_focus: "RECOVERY: Widen the slice frontier. Uncovered slices: {uncovered_slices}" }
585:           reset: { stuck_count: 0 }
586:         if_continue:
587:           log: "Iteration {current_iteration} complete. New agreement-eligible: {new_agreement_eligible_count}. Next focus: {next_focus}"
588:           set: { next_focus: "Advance the frontier: prioritize uncovered/low-agreement slices for the next shared sweep" }
589: 
590:       step_normalize_lifecycle_events:
591:         action: "Normalize pause and recovery lifecycle events to the frozen STOP_REASONS enum at emission time"
592:         stop_reasons_enum: [converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused]
593: 
594:       step_update_tracking:
595:         action: "Update loop tracking variables"
596:         set:
597:           current_iteration: "{current_iteration + 1}"
598:           stuck_count: "if new_agreement_eligible_count == 0 and merged_findings_count > 0: stuck_count + 1, else 0"
599:         log: "Iteration {N} complete. New agreement-eligible: {new_agreement_eligible_count}. sliceCoverage: {slice_coverage}. Stuck count: {stuck_count}."
600: 
601:       step_loop_decision:
602:         action: "Return to step_read_state for the next iteration (next parallel sweep)"
603:         goto: step_read_state
604: 
605:   # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
606:   # PHASE 3: SYNTHESIS
607:   # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
608:   phase_synthesis:
609:     purpose: Compile the merged findings into the reuse-first Context Report
610:     steps:
611: 
612:       step_hydrate_summary_metrics:
613:         action: "Hydrate summary metrics before synthesis"
614:         read:
615:           - "{state_paths.config}"
616:           - "{state_paths.state_log}"
617:           - "{state_paths.registry}"
618:         extract:
619:           - iteration_count: "Count lines where type === 'iteration'"
620:           - executor_pool_summary: "Pool roster from config (e.g. native×2; or native + cli seats for a custom pool)"
621:           - slice_coverage_final: "sliceCoverage from latest iteration record"
622:           - reuse_catalog_coverage_final: "reuseCatalogCoverage from latest iteration record"
623:           - agreement_rate_final: "agreementRate from latest iteration record"
624:           - relevance_floor_final: "relevanceFloor from latest iteration record"
625:           - dependency_completeness_final: "dependencyCompleteness from latest iteration record"
626:           - reason: "Latest stop reason if present, else 'converged' (member of STOP_REASONS enum)"
627: 
628:       step_read_registry_and_iterations:
629:         action: "Read the merged registry and all iteration files"
630:         read:
631:           - "{state_paths.registry}"
632:         read_pattern: "{state_paths.iteration_dir}/iteration-*.md"
633: 
634:       step_verify_citations:
635:         action: "Verify every cited file:symbol against the code graph before it enters the report"
636:         instructions: |
637:           For each reuse candidate / integration point / convention citation, confirm the file:symbol
638:           still resolves in the code graph (code_graph_query). Label verified | unverified.
639:           A stale reference is worse than omission — never ship an unlabeled stale citation.
640: 
641:       step_compile_report:
642:         action: "Compile the reuse-first Context Report (pointers, not source bodies)"
643:         template: .opencode/skills/deep-context/assets/context_report_template.md
644:         sections:
645:           - "Header: scope, executor pool, iteration count, stop reason"
646:           - "1. REUSE Catalog (highest value): id, symbol (file:line), signature, reuse verb, confidence, agreement (k/N), freshness, notes"
647:           - "2. Integration Points: [HARD]/[soft] entry/exit symbols with file:line"
648:           - "3. Touch List: files to change in implementation order"
649:           - "4. Conventions: file:line exemplars (naming, error handling, logging, test layout)"
650:           - "5. Pruned Dependency Subgraph: only edges within the touch radius"
651:           - "6. Prior Art / Decisions: related specs/ADRs/memory, date-stamped"
652:           - "7. Gaps & Unknowns: not-found/unverified + marginal-relevance near-misses"
653:           - "Methodology & Confidence: per-executor attribution, agreement counts, relevance gate, contradictions surfaced"
654:         output: "{state_paths.report_output}"
655: 
656:       step_emit_report_json:
657:         action: "Emit the machine-readable Context Report companion for downstream consumers (/speckit:plan, /speckit:implement)"
658:         instructions: |
659:           Write the same merged findings as structured JSON: reuseCandidates[], integrationPoints[],
660:           touchList[], conventions[], dependencySubgraph{}, priorArt[], gaps[], plus per-finding
661:           attribution {producedBy[], agreement, relevance, freshness} and the convergence signals.
662:         output: "{state_paths.report_json_output}"
663: 
664:       step_convergence_report:
665:         action: "Generate and append the convergence report to the Context Report"
666:         report: |
667:           ## Convergence Report
668:           - Stop reason: {reason}
669:           - Total iterations (parallel sweeps): {iteration_count}
670:           - Executor pool: {executor_pool_summary}
671:           - Final signals: sliceCoverage={slice_coverage_final}, reuseCatalogCoverage={reuse_catalog_coverage_final}, agreementRate={agreement_rate_final}, relevanceFloor={relevance_floor_final}, dependencyCompleteness={dependency_completeness_final}
672:           - Relevance gate: {relevance_gate} · Agreement min: {agreement_min} executors
673:           - Convergence threshold: {convergence_threshold}
674:         append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"synthesis_complete","mode":"context","totalIterations":{iteration_count},"stopReason":"{reason}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
675: 
676:       step_update_config_status:
677:         action: "Mark config as complete"
678:         edit: "{state_paths.config}"
679:         set_field: { status: "complete" }
680: 
681:       step_stage_artifact_dir:
682:         action: "Stage all skill-owned artifacts under artifact_dir for the operator's next git commit"
683:         command: "git add {state_paths.packet_dir}"
684:         note: "Staging the whole context packet keeps the audit trail intact (iterations/, deltas/, seats/, state.jsonl, strategy, report). Operators retain commit authority and can git restore --staged to unstage anything before commit. Failure is non-fatal."
685:         on_fail: "Log warning. Do not block synthesis. The artifact_dir may be outside a git repo (e.g., temp test fixture), which is valid."
686: 
687:   # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
688:   # PHASE 4: SAVE
689:   # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
690:   phase_save:
691:     purpose: Preserve context to the memory system
692:     steps:
693: 
694:       step_generate_context:
695:         action: "Route continuity content into the correct canonical spec document via generate-context.js"
696:         command: "node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js /tmp/save-context-data-<session-id>.json {spec_folder}"
697:         critical_rule: "NEVER manually author files under retired memory/ paths; use generate-context.js so the save routes into decision-record.md, implementation-summary.md, or handover.md"
698:         skip_when: "spec_folder is a standalone run dir outside specs/ or .opencode/specs/ (hand the report path to /speckit:plan instead)"
699: 
700:       step_index_memory:
701:         action: "Index the routed canonical spec document with memory_save when immediate MCP visibility is required"
702:         mcp_tool: memory_save
703:         invocation: 'memory_save({ filePath: "[routed-canonical-spec-doc-path]" })'
704:         note: "Use the actual canonical spec-doc path returned by generate-context.js; memory_index_scan remains the bulk fallback"
705: 
706:       step_verify_save:
707:         action: "Verify canonical save routed content into the correct spec document"
708:         check: "Does {spec_folder}/implementation-summary.md, {spec_folder}/decision-record.md, or {spec_folder}/handover.md carry the new continuity update?"
709:         on_fail: "Log warning but do not block completion"
710: 
711:       step_release_lock:
712:         action: "Release the single-writer lock after save, skip-save, or cancellation cleanup has resolved"
713:         command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock {state_paths.lock_file} --owner {captured_owner_pid}"
714:         append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"lock_released","path":"{state_paths.lock_file}","releasePhase":"save","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
715: 
716:   # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
717:   # COMPLETION
718:   # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
719:   completion:
720:     message: |
721:       Deep context complete.
722:       Iterations (parallel sweeps): {iteration_count} | Stop reason: {reason}
723:       Executor pool: {executor_pool_summary} | Final agreementRate: {agreement_rate_final}
724: 
725:       Artifacts:
726:         - context/context-report.md (reuse-first; pointers not bodies) + context/context-report.json
727:         - {iteration_count} iteration files in context/iterations/
728:         - Coverage-graph (loop_type=context) + state files: config.json, state.jsonl, strategy.md, findings-registry.json, dashboard.md
729:         - Memory context saved (when a spec folder was used)
730: 
731:       STATUS=OK PATH={spec_folder}
732:     next_steps:
733:       - "/speckit:plan [feature-description] (consume the reuse catalog + integration points + touch list)"
734:       - "/speckit:implement [spec-folder] (implement against the verified reuse map)"
735:       - "/memory:save {spec_folder} (refresh canonical continuity before the next session)"
736: 
737: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
738: # ERROR RECOVERY
739: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
740: error_recovery:
741:   seat_dispatch_timeout: "Record the seat as failed for the iteration; merge surviving seats (agreement degrades gracefully); retry the seat once next iteration."
742:   all_seats_failed: "After 3 consecutive empty sweeps, halt the loop and enter synthesis with partial findings (reason=error)."
743:   code_graph_unavailable: "Fall back to Glob+Grep frontier seeding and citation verification; label affected findings 'unverified'."
744:   provider_unauthenticated: "Drop the seat or escalate; never sweep with fewer executors than the agreement floor can satisfy without flagging it."
745:   state_file_missing: "If JSONL missing during loop, reconstruct from iteration files. If strategy missing, recreate a minimal version with the seeded frontier."
746:   memory_save_failure: "Preserve the context/ packet as backup. Log warning."
747: 
748: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
749: # RULES
750: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
751: rules:
752:   ALWAYS:
753:     - seed_frontier_before_first_sweep
754:     - dispatch_whole_pool_over_shared_focus_in_parallel
755:     - dispatch_native_seats_as_one_parallel_batch
756:     - host_writes_all_merged_state
757:     - merge_by_unit_id_with_per_executor_agreement
758:     - apply_relevance_gate_and_surface_contradictions
759:     - upsert_coverage_graph_loop_type_context
760:     - check_convergence_before_each_iteration
761:     - verify_cited_file_symbol_against_code_graph
762:     - ship_pointers_not_source_bodies
763:     - respect_max_iterations_cap
764:   NEVER:
765:     - sweep_the_whole_repo
766:     - dispatch_native_seats_sequentially_one_at_a_time
767:     - let_a_seat_write_merged_state
768:     - count_files_visited_as_progress
769:     - silently_resolve_contradictions
770:     - paste_raw_source_into_the_report
771:     - register_new_mcp_tools
772:     - skip_convergence_check

(End of file - total 772 lines)
